Harsh Srivastava.author: Scott
Harsh Srivastava.text: First we lay the foundation, I teach them how to set goals through deep journaling. You can't get where you want to go if you don't know for starters where you want to go. Through deep journaling you also teach yourself to think in an orderly, structured and logical manner and can develop actionable steps to achieve your goals, basically reverse engineer such goals into daily habits and tasks. That's where the timetable comes in. I teach them how to build their timetables to get consistent at the habits that will take them from where they are to where they want to go. Usually we all want a strong aesthetic body and be healthy, have energy and vitality, so I teach them how to get consistent at the gym and the secrets of nutrition (must people are clueless about nutrition, for example most people think vegetables are healthy but vegetables are filled with anti-nutrients like oxalates and phytic acid, oxalates bind to iron, zing and magnesium in your blood and render them useless for you body to assimilate, that's why they are called anti-nutrients).

Harsh Srivastava.author: Scott
Harsh Srivastava.text: We all want wisdom and knowledge to make sound decisions and to know which path to take, so I teach them to get consistent and reading which is the most profound way to acquire knowledge there is.

Harsh Srivastava.author: Scott
Harsh Srivastava.text: We all want to have good mental health, control over out attention and a personal understanding of the structure of reality, that's why I teach them how to meditate and eleveate their vibrational frequency. How to do gratitude journaling consistently and to expand their consciousness.

Harsh Srivastava.author: Scott
Harsh Srivastava.text: And last but no least we need a purpose, a way, a path, a mission. I teach them how to find it and follow it. Build their online businesses, help other people (potentially as educational content creators) and once they are ready and if I see they are worthy I take them into my inner circle and get them ready for the next stage. I'm putting together a team of high quality, powerful men into self-improvement making online more than $10K per month to kickstart a civilizational project, at the beginning a nomadic tribe of men into self-improvement off the grid, soverign. But to get into that place they need first to overcome The Trial. The initiation ritual. A 3 months journey by sea from England to Karelia, northen Russia, where we'll hunt wolves and each men will have to undergo a series of tests to prove he can become part of our brotherhood.

Miguel Ariza Lora.author: Miguel Ariza Lora
Miguel Ariza Lora.text: What's up gang,My name is Miguel, 25 years old and Spanish-Dutch currently living in Thailand.My journey in self-improvement began around 15 years old when I was overweight. I really wanted to be more like the others my age. Luckily this is when YouTube began to take off and people starting sharing a lot of value around fitness and nutrition. Trial and error got me to losing 20kg's eventually, and I've been able to keep most of it off ever since. When I saw putting some work in could give me results, I realized the same thing could be done in other aspects.The following years I became a lot more extroverted, social and confident. Through studying social work and psychology I got into coaching people to build a better life. I was good doing those jobs, and now I've taken it online and moved to Thailand. Felt like a good next step in my self-improvement. Have been coaching people now for 7 years already. Learned a whole lot. About people, training techniques, but probably even more about myself.I just think the self-improvement journey is amazing, and I wish everyone can experience seeing themselves improve into a better version and better life, step by step. Does it ever end? I don't think so. There's always room for improvement. It can feel tiring at times, but I wouldn't want to have it any other way.Can't wait to connect more with all of you guys.- Miguel

Miguel Ariza Lora.author: Scott Northwolf
Miguel Ariza Lora.text: @Thomas El Nadir brother, quite an epic story. What a journey! So many of us start motivated by one of the most basic desires we men have and that leads us to discover ourselves and start improving, some start with the gym, some start with game and social skills but in the end we all arrive at the same holistic understanding if we stay consistent on our paths for long enough.Really glad to have you here with us, brother. This is truly were you belong. And now it's time to build something truly wonderful with all the wisdom we have accumulated and give back to the world that has been so abundantly generous to us.

Miguel Ariza Lora.author: Thomas El Nadir
Miguel Ariza Lora.text: @Scott Northwolf Thanks man!Yes. Give, give, give and make the world a better place until receiving is just a natural consequence of living in this better place we created 🔥

Miguel Ariza Lora.author: Scott Northwolf
Miguel Ariza Lora.text: Welcome to the gang, Bruno. Glad to have you with us, brother!One of the biggest things for me was when I stopped thinking about what I wanted and started thinking in terms of what other people wanted and needed.Choosing a niche, going to them and actually asking them consistently and in a methodical way what were their dreams and hopes and what were their biggest problems and using that to build a truly attractive offer that they'd resonated with and they actually wanted to buy and then coming back and offering that to them.And if you picked right the niche and you were able to find them to conduct niche research interviews with them... you'll be able to find them again on the same place and get them on sales' calls with you.Then all that remains is to conduct appointment setting, sales' calls, build a good script and funnel structure and track your KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) in order to improve over time, stay consistent and don't switch niches or business models all together because that resets your progress back to 0.

Miguel Ariza Lora.text: @Miguel Ariza Lora since 2023. And got into self-improvement in 2021.

Justin Yip.author: Scott
Justin Yip.text: I basically base my methodology on what made Sam Ovens a millionaire (and many other people). Good product to market fit, ensured by proper niche selection, niche research phase, then building an offer packaged and marketed correctly (basically using the dream outcome, most painful percieved problem and language of the niche), developing a methodology that actually delivers results, guranteeing such result with a risk reversal guarantee, installing a solid and proven sales' system (propper appointment setting, solid and consistent outreach with cold and warm outbound, a sales' script built leveraging the huristics of the human mind and sales' call recording and reviewing system for reiteration applying the scientific method to business for consistent and effective "AB testing")...

Justin Yip.author: Scott
Justin Yip.text: then (and what people underrestimate the most but actually accounts for the 70% of people's success at business) mindset training to ensure emotional control and capacity to actually do the work consistently even when you are getting no apparent results... like I said... it's simple once you know it, understand it and have done it, but it's not easy, most people just give up too easily and without the proper guidence, accountability and pressure to perform they just never make it and keep changing from one thing to the other... the grass is always greener on the other side, right?

Arthur Maddocks-Brown.author: Hanna Urban
Arthur Maddocks-Brown.text: Hi everyone,For me it goes like this:.It started with a doctor directing me to meditation and yoga instead of prescribing medications. (Yes, there are luckily doctors like that 🙏)Years ago I was suffering from depression and that was the beginning of it all for meThe more I dove into this journey of physical connection (yoga had more and faster effect on me), the more I felt at home. I started discovering spirituality form totally different angle and started to broaden my view.I started in 2021 but it wasn't a straight journey (i guess it never is...😅)Fully on board, restarting my business as I took needed time after my father's death

Arthur Maddocks-Brown.author: Scott Northwolf
Arthur Maddocks-Brown.text: @Arthur Maddocks-Brown infinite gratitude for sharing again, brother, your story is truly inspiring and I love the fact that you took the time to write it down twice on both places, that's what I call commitment!

Arthur Maddocks-Brown.author: Scott Northwolf
Arthur Maddocks-Brown.text: @Arthur Maddocks-Brown I couldn't agree more, brother.

Arthur Maddocks-Brown.author: Arthur Maddocks-Brown
Arthur Maddocks-Brown.text: Hey Guys!You may have seen my other post... so I PROMISE to keep this one shorter!After 15 years of depression and suicidal thoughts, the loss of my wife, house, dog, job, life, friends (everything) I hit rock bottom.I had a choice.Die or LiveIn that momentI chose to live..Not just EXISTBut Fucking LIVE!!!I studied life coaching and got 2 x certifications (Jay Shetty and Expert Coaching certifications)I got my breathwork, trauma and nervous system informed certificates as well as cold water immersion certification.When I started learning about coaching, in order to get my qualifications, I needed to coach people. I took what I learnt and started coaching (internationally)I fell in love with helping people. It made me feel good and it helped me heal.I've been in the coaching space now for 3 years!I am still in the building phase, but it's growing and opportunities are popping up.As soon as I got out my way... these things started to flow better.. I certainly could do better though!

Arthur Maddocks-Brown.author: Scott Northwolf

Appreciate you sharing with all of us, brother. I'm really glad to welcome you into our wonderful community! This is the very beginning of a truly EPIC journey and I'm honored by your presence here to allow me to be part of it for you, Jamie.Let's start building together!

Arthur Maddocks-Brown.text: Hey Guys!  I am on my own hero's journey!  I am a writer, so I will keep it short & upload a video to help you meet me better!  1. Aged 15 diagnosed with depression, lived the next 15 years with depression and suicide ideation, multiple times a day I would have consistent and persistent thoughts.  2. In 2019 I got engaged to my partner of 7 years (aged 27)  3. We got a dog (2019) 4. COVID happened (2020)  5. We bought some land (2020) 6. October 2020 I was hit by a car   7. We built a house, got married, moved cities and changed jobs (2021)  8. 2021 cracks started forming in the relationship  9. 2022 Christmas I broke down about my mental heath challenges, my physical rehab journey and...  10. 2023 (January) she told me she was leaving...  11. I nearly took my life... pills left over from my car accident in 2020... but my dog walked in the room and I said fuck... I can't do that to you.  12. 2023 the house was sol

Kamil A.author: Kamil A
Kamil A.text: Hey, I'm Kamil. I've been on self-improvement for years and became a self-improvement coach this January. I got on self-improvement to leave my depression-inducing uni routine. And it did help me; I dropped out and moved to another country.  I used to work as a copywriter for a couple years. It paid well but was really unfulfilling in my eyes. I tried different things til I figured out what I wanted to do was coaching.  I'm in Georgia (in Europe) right now but moving to Bali as soon as I build stable income from coaching.

Kamil A.author: Scott Northwolf
Kamil A.text: Self-Improvement is truly the cure to depression (after only two weeks that I built my timetable and started meditating, reading and working out consistently I overcame a 4 years long chronic depression).I find it truly crazy how in the Western world and the US especially they just don't talk about it, they just diagnose you with depression and fill you up with pills instead of actually helping you with the right knowlege.Really glad I got into this path.Thanks for sharing your experience, brother.Welcome to the gang!

Aeon Bancuyo.author: Aeon Bancuyo
Aeon Bancuyo.text: I have all of this problems

Aeon Bancuyo.author: Scott Northwolf
Aeon Bancuyo.text: @Aeon Bancuyo alright! So, let's break it down:You are afraid of charging high ticket.Why is that?Is it because you feel like your services are no valuable enough?Then tell me...How much do you feel comfortable with charging (no number is wrong, even if it's 0 at the moment)?Now...whatever that number is...Think...how can you make that number 10 times higher?What kind of MASSIVE value could you provide in order to make it worth that amount of money for people?That's a first step towards reframing this limiting belief.It's a basic framework but it will give you an idea of how much you can improve in order to be able to charge the kind of prices that would not just change your life but your clients' lives as well, because the higher ticket they pay the higher the percieved value they'll see in your offer, the higher their commitment and the higher their likelihood of achieving their desired outcome.The second question is a two part thing.First comes clarity in what you offer and then comes the confidence to state the value behind it.Without that clarity is very hard to express confidently how much are you charging for it, because it's hard to see the value when things are chaotic and confusing and confidence is nothing but a proxy for competence.So we start by actually having a VERY well defined outcome (that aligns with the percieved pain/desired of your prospects) enticing enough for the people in your niche (of course, there are certain foundations to this like picking properly the niche, conducting niche research interviews to actually understand in depth the percieved pain your potential clients have and their desired outcome, but I'll be talking in more depth about the roadmap to $10K per month that we follow here on self-improvement Nation soon to make all of this more clear, I've been asked already a lot for this and it's going to be very useful to our members who haven't spoke to me frequently).With regards to the third one.The fact that you are afraid of paying for mentorship is not really about mentors out there (even though obviusly like in all market there are good ones and shittier ones) but it speaks about how you view yourself and ties back to the fear of having people paying you for your services because you don't believe yet your services are that valuable or perhaps that you can deliever effectively, and if you don't believe (yet) in your own coaching... how can you believe in someone else's?This is just the beginning but I hope it does offers you some clarity and makes you reflect in depth about this subject.I'd recommend deep journaling about it and if you have any questions feel free to reach out, Aeon.Like I said, there's a lot more but take it step by step. It's all about consistency.Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called faith, brother! Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

Aeon Bancuyo.author: Aeon Bancuyo
Aeon Bancuyo.text: (edited)@Scott Northwolf thanks so much for this man! you really care for us, will indeed do deep journaling

Aeon Bancuyo.author: Scott Northwolf

Aeon Bancuyo.author: Aeon Bancuyo
Aeon Bancuyo.text: I’m curious do you have friends or do you interact often with people not on self improvement?  I thought about the picture of the about page that @Scott Northwolf has, and I would love to have that kind of friend group as well.  Honestly, I’ve been in pain my whole life for not having a “stable” friend group, is this normal?  My friends circle we’re only 3 at a time, and it would die off overtime.  Currently, having not deep relationships with my school friends.  There’s so many people who I can be friends with in school, it’s too easy.  But I have a limiting belief that, theyre not worth it. Honestly, I get the “ick” when I’m trying to fit in to the gossip conversations, or the shallow “issue” of the day.  What do you do to battle this, I have a two friends that I have a deep relationship with but not at school.  What do you do to keep being social, even with people outside of self imorovemnt?35Last comment Jan 29

Aeon Bancuyo.author: Scott Northwolf
Aeon Bancuyo.text: I'm not really social with people who are not into self-improvement to be honest. The beauty about Bali is that EVERYONE (or at least most people) are into self-improvement so that makes it perfect but I know this is a very rare place, that's also the reason I moved here.Back in the day, when I was still back home I had a big group of friends but when I got into self-improvement I just stopped having things in common with them, our interests just weren't the same and even though I tried to get them on self-improvement I failed. You cannot force people to improve their lives if they don't want to.So basically I endured the time in solitude and used it to not be afraid of being alone and carve my own path, I also used it as motivation to make a shit ton of money and start traveling the world in order to find my tribe which I successfully did here in Bali.On self-improvement you'll go through this phase of loneliness in which you are not high status enough yet to be friends with the people you want to be friends in real life because they are still way above you but if you use that as motivation to get to their level you will get there, it's just a matter of time and commitment.I know it's hard but it's worth it and there's no going back once you take the red pill anyways.The only option is to keep moving forward, brother. Use this community to remind yourself of the people you'll have around you when you make it to the other side and don't lose faith.A lot of us are living here already and we have fulfilled the dream we were shooting for... now we are building together towards a higher aim and I can tell you there's nothing better than making your dreams come true with effort, sacrifice and dedication.

Aeon Bancuyo.author: Aeon Bancuyo
Aeon Bancuyo.text: @Scott Northwolf thanks for this man!I'll keep this as a reminder, but do you think there's a way for me to be not a social nerd and just make myself isolated, whilst on self improvement?

Aeon Bancuyo.author: Scott Northwolf
Aeon Bancuyo.text: @Aeon Bancuyo the funny thing about social skills is that you can actually develop them more by developing yourself and creating a rock solid confidence born out of REAL competence than by simply "doing reps" and talking to normies non-stop. It's the highest leverage thing you can do. By developing this sense of pride on yourself and having high standards for the people around you, counter-intuitively you become magnetic to people. I know the instant gratification pull of being social is strong but I can promise you that locking in and developing yourself as a man will only bring net benefits to your life and will not turn you into a "social weirdo" or a "self-improvement nerd" umless you hold such limiting belief and turn it into a self fulfilling profecy.

Aeon Bancuyo.author: Andrew Brooks
Aeon Bancuyo.text: I'm not really "social" either. I have about 3 good friends in my locale, and I have others that I connect with virtually every week. But I do feel the need to be social often.So, I invite friends over for dinner, plant to meet up for a coffee or a movie, or just meet up to hang out somewhere for an hour. It doesn't have to be a big thing.But I do plan fun things intentionally. I keep track of the artsy/cultural things happening in my area and will often just show up to some of those and see if I can chat with a new person there. And I will sometimes invite people I meet to connect with me at the event. Sometimes they show, sometimes not. But I'm ok doing things by myself too.So don't feel weird. You aren't. Especially as we get older, the number of friends typically dwindles, but the QUALITY of the friendships goes up. And that's ok.

Aeon Bancuyo.author: Scott Northwolf
Aeon Bancuyo.text: @Andrew Brooks very grounded approach.

Scott I know you get messages like this all time lol

But hey welcome to The Founders Club!

Feel free to ask any questions in the group for other members to help.

If you want my personal help, check out the details for my Inner Circle👇
[https://dropservice.io/skool-ic](https://dropservice.io/skool-ic)

Btw what made you look into this?

Renzo Guevarra.author: Scott
Renzo Guevarra.text: I started making money online around 6 months ago. I knew for a fact since the very beginning I was not going to work a regular job in my life. I always wanted more, and if the goal of working was money you would never win such game working for someone, it was always going to be a business, and there is no higher leverage business than an online business. Also, I'm not just going to do anything just for the money, I can move to fucking Tibet and chill with the monks and money would never be an issue, I'm in business to help people and make a meaningful impact on the world, I want to build a legacy. I bet you don't get responses like this all the time.

Siebe De Gelas.author: Scott
Siebe De Gelas.text: That girl, man! Half German, hald Indonesian, born and raised in Germany. I can say they fed her well there... no wonder she's so big on IG, I'd love to see that face every day myself... but the house was filled with girls like that and BETTER. That shit was like Love Island or one of those crazy dumb TV shows they used to put on the small screen. I ended up entangled with 3 of them at the house. And tomorrow one of the wants to see me so badly that she video-called me tonight out of nowhere and when I told her that tomorrow I was busy all day working she said that it's fine, she'll come over regardless just to watch me work... WILD! I love these girls... ALL OF THEM! All these little bunnies around me but there's only one carrot and they all got to share it! 🤣 Have I mentioned how grateful I am for my life? To say that I'm living the dream would be an underestatment at this point. Still even though the journey is perfect and everything is truly wonderful I'll keep reaching for more... there's a higher calling for me. Comfort and pleasure will not numb me or seduce me... there's a deep fire burning within my soul and it shall be release for the glory of my people. I'm putting together this tribe and nothing will stand in our way. Greatness is coming...

She'll come to me... they all do in the end... they all do. I'm the fucking CENTER of Bali, bro... everything seems to spin around me in this island... I don't even know what I'm doing but something I must be doing right! 😂

Siebe De Gelas.author: Scott
Siebe De Gelas.text: I love this place, man. Everyone seems to be in love with me. I have no clue why! Well... I have a couple of ideas... but still... no distractions... I'm focusing on the community, the upcoming weekly calls, bringing more people in, having more sales' calls, more niche research interviews as well, closing more deals, staying focus, delivering like crazy! All I care about is the MISSION. All these nice things, people, attention, the events... they are all secondary and only in my life as long as they serve the cause. Ironclaw discipline! Let's fucking goooooooooo! We are building the future, man. This is growing like crazy! I'm blowing up on other people's instagrams all around Bali. Being surrounded by so many influencers.... basically being the IRL influencer of the influencers makes you famous by fucking accident... 🤣

Siebe De Gelas.author: Scott
Siebe De Gelas.text: I'm telling you... Bali is magical. There's nothing you can't do here. Everything gets manifested at such a breakneck speed that you can barely adapt to all the things that are going on around you.

Siebe De Gelas.author: Scott
Siebe De Gelas.text: We get only what we believe we deserve. If you believe you deserve to enjoy Bali only when you have built a course you will hold that limiting belief until the moment you finally feel ready to break it. It's like when people say "Oh, when I make enough money I'll retired and then I'll be happy." Usually they retire and die in less than a year after that... it's what happened to my uncle and I have seen it a million times. Be aware of that trap. Distractions here only get you if you allow them. Nothing happens without your consent, either conscious or unconscious.

Siebe De Gelas.author: Lead

Siebe De Gelas.author: Scott

It's usually the only thing that can be... even if we are talking about not being motivated enough because of comfort and not a strong pain to move faster... a limiting belief is lurking there in the shadows (potentially). And the only way to overcome it (if there's any) is to bring it into the light.

Kai Cerar.author: Kai Cerar

Kai Cerar.author: Scott Northwolf
Kai Cerar.text:
@Kai Cerar one of the most powerful things this can do for you is first of all shifting the mindset from the victim's perspective to the main character's perspective.
How would your life change if you knew that EVRYTHING that happens in your life is not happening TO you but FOR you and that you have chosen it all from your higher perspective in order for you to learn a lesson and grow through awareness.
Wouldn't it lift a huge weight from your shoulders to start 'playing' at life instead of struggling though it?
Detatchment from outcome creates a kind of confident and playful relaxation that allows us to face challenges with an unexorable and relentless mental fortitude...
This allows us to actually dream big and set huge goals but also reverse engineer them into actionable steps that we can undertake daily in order to acheive such dreams.
If you want you can share what's you ultimate goal for your life and we can map it into your current day to day to see how you are making progress consistently towards such goal.

Kai Cerar.author: Kai Cerar
Kai Cerar.text: @Scott Northwolf bro this is beautiful thanks!!

Kai Cerar.author: Kai Cerar
Kai Cerar.text: @Scott Northwolf my ultimate life goal honestly changes a lot day by day. I’m just enjoying the process, but I do think that a vision is very important.

Kai Cerar.author: Scott Northwolf
Kai Cerar.text:
@Kai Cerar maybe taking one full day to do a full-blown dopamine detox and journal very deeply about what you want out of this life will help.
I do an exercise with my clients for goal setting which is VERY powerful.
We humans are like ships, we set sail and go into the ocean, a lot of people end up stranded on a desert island wondering why they didn't become millionaires or something like that (let's say becoming a millionaire would be in this metaphor arriving at Bristol, England and let's say you set sail from Boston).
Well... did you ever set a goal to actually get there?
Imagine a ship going into the ocean without compass, without a map or a port of destination...
Hard to see meaning on the voyage if you don't really know where you are going.
Enjoying the process is a very important part of it but having a powerful motivation is a game changer.

Kai Cerar.author: Kai Cerar
Kai Cerar.text: @Scott Northwolf facts

Kai Cerar.author: Kai Cerar

Kai Cerar.author: James Bargeron
Kai Cerar.text:
@Scott Northwolf - agree, we all need a compass if we set that path, otherwise we end up drifting (book Outwitting the Devil) - and yes, enjoying the process, but for me, motivation is an exterior driver and not something that I believe comes from our soul.
When I was a kid, it was a teacher who used a wooden hockey stick on the back of exposed legs to motivate me. These days I think inspiration is a much more powerful driver, which in itself comes from the source, In-spirit. Which feels more aligned for me.

Lucas Oliveira.text: Mannn I just saw your story on synthesiser that is SO cool to see your transformation. What is the biggest thing you have improved since you started changing your life?

Lucas Oliveira.author: Scott
Lucas Oliveira.text: There's so many things... but probably the most powerful thing that changed was my mind. My worldview. Realizing that absolute and utter personal responsability is the straightest path to power, because power is directly proportional to responsability. Understanding that life is a game, so it makes no sense to sit around and play it safe, it makes all the sense in the world to take amssive risks, to always bet on yourself, to get rid of limiting beliefs and forgo the rules set by other men, take what you want when you want and if you can't get it then figure out how to.

Lucas Oliveira.author: Scott
Lucas Oliveira.text: Teach yourself to think, deeply and ligically from first principles by learning the ancient art of reflection and deep journaling. Reverse engineer your goals into daily habits and build a timetable that gets you every day closer to them. Progress is food for your brain. It raises your seretonine levels. It makes you feel good. Be mindful of what you consume, you are furnishing your unconscious mind. If you consume trash that trash will surfice into your conscious and you'll make bad decisions, not because you are dumb, your brain is the most powerful and beautiful machine ever created by the universe, but the problem is that you have been feeding it bad fuel, and it shows on the quality of your decisions.

Lucas Oliveira.author: Scott
Lucas Oliveira.text: Thank you by the way. I'm really glad you found it inspiring.

Lucas Oliveira.author: Lead
Lucas Oliveira.text: Man that's so poweful and I completely agree with everything you said. I teach people how to get in shape even with limited time in life. And my unique mechanism behind it is to show them how to be disciplined with this new habit. How to keep the consistency long term to bring the results they want. And I always tell my clients the power of controling emotions, how our subcouncious mind controls us and what we need to do to achieve what we are looking for. Putting ourselves in umconfortable situations is what will take us to the next level.

Jack Shiller.text:
𝗔𝗶 𝗦𝗹𝗼𝗽.. 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗗𝗲𝗮𝗱 𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗻𝗲𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗼𝗿𝘆 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗦𝘆𝗻𝘁𝗵𝗲𝘀𝗶𝘇𝗲𝗿 𝗣𝗼𝘀𝘁𝘀..
Yeah I said it Synthesizer is being filled with articles written by BOTS! 🤖
Funniest thing I've seen is 2 articles back to back written by two separate people, same message almost the exact wording. (Not funny at all actually)
The information in this place used to be 𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘀𝗲, 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗿𝗮𝘄..
More value per syllable than any other community..
But now its being littered with em dashes, regurgitated nonsense and oh yea my favorite one, intellectual parroting (sarcasm).
The dead internet theory is a concept predicting that the internet will become predominantly composed of AI-generated content and bot activity, rather than human interaction..
The sad part is that WE are spreading this ai slop. (Literally doing the bid of AI).
By increasing the pace at which it swallows, LITERALLY engulfs the internet whole.
We are masquerading.
Devaluing our own authenticity.
Impersonating what it's like to be a human.
I want to share that community is built on connection and connection is real and raw.
And RAW makes mistakes. Those mistakes in the moment to you, might seem like flaws or blemishes that should be covered. But I want to argue that it is those blemishes are what will distinguish YOU from all of the other articles, posts and NOW 𝗔𝗜- 𝗚𝗘𝗡𝗘𝗥𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗 𝗦𝗟𝗢𝗣 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗚𝗥𝗔𝗠𝗦.. <-- (Can you believe that ?!)
⭐Studies now show that we even speak like ai ⭐
Tell me if you've heard this.
"Not only is this not x, But its Y"
"Its not x but Y"
And words like DELVE 🤣😆😂
The Rule of Threes. 𝗘𝘅. ( Clear, concise, and compelling) (I DONT even feel right using the rule of threes in formulating my writing because I think people will perceive it as AI. (Even though I speak like that naturally as a philosophy reader)
Its naturally rolling off of our tongues at this point because we consume it SO OFTEN.
ONLY increasing the very PROBLEM that this community aims to solve in the first place..
🌊"𝗪𝗲 𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗱𝗿𝗼𝘄𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝗻 𝗶𝗻𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗯𝘂𝘁 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝘄𝗶𝘀𝗱𝗼𝗺"🌊
With every article generated by ai you are effectively adding MORE water to the already filled bucket.
I want to share with you that not polishing an article, post or comments is actually going to 𝗵𝗲𝗹𝗽 𝘆𝗼𝘂, 𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗿 𝘆𝗼𝘂.
🤮Articles on social media migration and social media rebellion states: "THEY ARE LEAVING BECAUSE IT NO LONGER 𝗙𝗘𝗘𝗟𝗦 𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟" SAYING IT 𝗙𝗘𝗘𝗟𝗦 𝗔𝗥𝗧𝗜𝗙𝗜𝗖𝗜𝗔𝗟, 𝗨𝗡𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟 𝗔𝗡𝗗 𝗠𝗔𝗡𝗜𝗣𝗨𝗟𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗.. "IT FEELS LIKE A 𝗙𝗔𝗞𝗘 𝗘𝗡𝗩𝗜𝗢𝗥𝗡𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗧 𝗖𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗 𝗕𝗬 𝗔 𝗠𝗔𝗖𝗛𝗜𝗡𝗘" ..🤮
So with all that in mind..
Think before you use ai.
Think before you let it write your articles for you.
And for god sakes don't let it write your whole program from conception to birth, so you can COLD pitch it to someone FOR money.
𝗧𝗛𝗔𝗧 𝗶𝘀 𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝗯𝗲𝗮𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗳𝘂𝗹 𝗺𝗼𝗻𝗲𝘆. 𝗧𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝘀𝗰𝗮𝗺.
A NOT so clever scam that aims to take advantage of people who are in pain and need experts, 𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟 𝗦𝗬𝗡𝗧𝗛𝗘𝗦𝗜𝗭𝗘𝗥𝗦, 𝗡𝗢𝗧 𝗔𝗜 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗺𝗽𝘁 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗳𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹𝘀.

Jack Shiller.author: Scott Northwolf
Jack Shiller.text:
Bro, I agree completely. I am a writter at heart and you and I have this beautiful obsession with knowledge and understanding, and I know you draw as much satisfaction from teaching and sharing with others your knowledge as I do... and it is quite literally like a drug for us... but in a good sense, because we get to connect very deeply with amazing people and we get to touch their lives in very impactful ways changing it for the better.
I'm really glad you are bringing this to people's attention because this is something I ALWAYS say to people: If you let AI write for you... I mean... can you really take pride on what "you wrote"? It's not even your own knowledge... and it's sad... because by teaching we solidify our knowledge.... by teaching we learn at the same time.
This is what we are here for and letting something as beautiful as that die would be a true shame.
At the same time... I don't think the AI slop is so bad. It's actually WONDERFUL! Because the more of it there is out there the more we will stand out for being authentically ourseleves and the more rare we'll be. We'll be the only ones willing to do the "hard work" of writting and teaching ourselves and we will benefit greatly personally from that.
The work works more on you than you work on it, and that's a fact, so when people chose the easy path, the path of least resistance they are missing out on the amazing gift that focus and deep work bring to their lives, the development of their discipline, the development of their capacity to actually sit in presence on the present moment and give their best to something beautiful and meaningful like it is connecting with people and helping them change their lives.
I truly loved this post. Thank you so much for sharing it with me, brother... and above all: thank you for being you, Jason, you are a shinning lighthouse in the middle of this dark sea and you have no idea of how deeply appreciated you are, man.
Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion, but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brother! Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

Jack Shiller.author: Scott Northwolf
Jack Shiller.text:
@Jack Shiller yeah, when I came across it for the first time I thought the same and I saw some traditions differing about it but i opted for the model of the Ancient Greeks (Pythagorians, Platonists, Neo-Platonists) and the Hermetics (as well as the Mediaval Alchemists, but those guys went crazy overcomplicating the whole thing)...
They explain that the structure is the following:
1-Monad (source, void).
2-Duat (Mental plain).
3-Triad (Causal plain: it would sound logical that if you have two things you can see cause an effect but in reality is not possible because if there's not a third 'thing', an observer then there's no choice to be made, there's no distinction between you and the other thing because there's no awareness of you as such if there's no separation, if there's no YOU, let's say, and also an awareness or consiousness component within you that is observing yourself and the other 'thing' with which you are interacting then there's no causality, I hope I explained myself correctly there).
4-Tetrad (Astral plain, the inifinte realm of possibilities, the quantum field of possibilities, ideas, concepts, emotions, imaginations, all that we can percieve through the third eye).
5-Pentad (Physical plain, 'material' reality, what Buddhists call Maya, illusion, the realm we percieve through our senses).
PS: That's the Tree of Life representing the Ten Sefirot from the Jewish Kabbalah. The Jewish Kabbalah is an esoteric tradition taken from the ancient Egyptian Prists and Farohs by Moses and revealed trough him to the Jewish people. It's good stuff. Most pathways lead to the top of the mountain. From the top the view is always the same.

Jack Shiller.author: Jack Shiller
Jack Shiller.text: @Scott Northwolf I'm with you on that order, it's maybe a "terminology" difference for the Duat. But that basically sums up the formless --> form there

Jack Shiller.author: Jack Shiller
Jack Shiller.text: I love this Scott. And your humility is admirable

Jack Shiller.author: Jack Shiller
Jack Shiller.text:
Sometimes I feel scared of making a banger post to a community if I know that it could fade away into the feed and get forgotten 👶
Yet in many ways, I kinda like that.
On Skool, and especially in Synthesizer, posting to the community feels like it’s based off of the unique energy of the day.
It’s less about how polished the writing is, and more about how attuned it is to the community atmosphere at any given moment.
I try not to be excessive with my moderator powers here in Synthesizer lmao but..
Thought leadership posts fail, honest call-to-action questions are appreciated, and posts that spark conversation work best.
These guidelines help us focus on what’s in front of us TODAY and NOW. Not on what’s in the future.
But it makes me wonder what we can do with those posts that we're proud of, once they've been swept away into the feed.
Asking u guys...
💭 Do you repurpose any of your Skool posts for other content?

Jack Shiller.author: Scott Northwolf
Jack Shiller.text:
The work works more on you than you work on it... by simply writing it you are solidifying the knowledge in your mind and the more you do it the stronger those core teachings become to your toolkit of critical knowledge to deply and help people and more people will see it the more persistent your are in your endeavour of spreading such message and you will be extremely persistent if you truly believe that such knowledge has transformed your life and can potentially transform others.
So I'm quite detatched from the outcome of the process of me sharing my knowledge, I simply do it and if nobody listens that's fine... eventually the right person will come across it if I just keep putting it out there and will benefit massively from it and it'll all be worth it in the end.
That's my approach to it.

Jack Shiller.author: Jack Shiller
Jack Shiller.text:
@Scott Northwolf I love that bro. What you said is really oriented around the inner growth involved with the creative process. I think the desire to have it be "listened to" is strong at times, but whoever needs to see it will see it.
Someone in my community had just told me that even if my post touches one person, it's an enormous win.

Jack Shiller.author: Scott Northwolf
Jack Shiller.text: @Jack Shiller exactly!

Jack Shiller.author: Harry Montford
Jack Shiller.text: We have 4 main deep work archetypes.  These are like the 4 classes you can pick inside of a video game. Each have their own benefits, buffs and weaknesses.  They are: - Monastic: 1 main task that you work on for most of the day, minimal breaks. - Bidomal: Long deep work days, but also complete rest days inside the week.  - Rhythmic: Deep work sessions in the morning, shallow work in the afternoon -> evening.  - Journalistic: No clear structure, one minute you could be partying in Bali, the next you are recording          a YT video.  My personal favourite is Rhythmic, but I also know many people who prefer the Monastic work type.  SO here's my question, what would do you follow and which one would you LIKE to try?

Jack Shiller.author: Scott Northwolf
Jack Shiller.text: Bro, I'm in Bali but I never do the Journalist style, lol 😂.For me is either Monastic or Rhythmic and I honestly prefer Monastic but it's truly an art and quite a skill to develop.

Jack Shiller.author: Scott

Jack Shiller.author: Scott
Jack Shiller.text:
Step 5: Reverse Engineer The Numbers

$ Goal ➗ Client value = Clients required

Clients required ➗ SCR = Good fit calls required

Good fit calls required ➗ GFR = Show ups required

Show ups required ➗ SUR = Bookings required

Bookings required ➗ ABR = Outreach volume required

Outreach volume required ➗ Days to achieve goal = Daily Activity Required

** all metrics should be stated as a decimal, e.g. 30% = 0.30

Samuel Hill.author: Scott
Samuel Hill.text: Bro, I'm telling you, my consistency this last 3 years has been mainly due to the time table, without that it's almost impossible to stay really consistent. Think of it more like a guideline for your day instead of like a prison. You don't really have to complete every single one of the habits on the timetable but if you focus on the priorities and make sure to check the timetable every time you feel kind of lost throughout the day... trust me you will crush it and eventually you'll have built unshakable habits that stick for ever and turn into part of your identity.

Dr. Stephen Buscema.author: Scott
Dr. Stephen Buscema.text:
# Stage 1:

First we lay the foundation, I teach them how to set goals through deep journaling. You can't get where you want to go if you don't know for starters where you want to go. Through deep journaling you also teach yourself to think in an orderly, structured and logical manner and can develop actionable steps to achieve your goals, basically reverse engineer such goals into daily habits and tasks. That's where the timetable comes in. I teach them how to build their timetables to get consistent at the habits that will take them from where they are to where they want to go.

# Stage 2:

Usually we all want a strong aesthetic body and be healthy, have energy and vitality, so I teach them how to get consistent at the gym and the secrets of nutrition (must people are clueless about nutrition, for example most people think vegetables are healthy but vegetables are filled with anti-nutrients like oxalates and phytic acid, oxalates bind to iron, zing and magnesium in your blood and render them useless for you body to assimilate, that's why they are called anti-nutrients).

We all want wisdom and knowledge to make sound decisions and to know which path to take, so I teach them to get consistent and reading which is the most profound way to acquire knowledge there is.

We all want to have good mental health, control over out attention and a personal understanding of the structure of reality, that's why I teach them how to meditate and eleveate their vibrational frequency. How to do gratitude journaling consistently and to expand their consciousness.

# Stage 3:

And last but no least we need a purpose, a way, a path, a mission. I teach them how to find it and follow it. Build their online businesses, help other people (potentially as educational content creators) and once they are ready and if I see they are worthy I take them into my inner circle and get them ready for the next stage.

# Stage 4:

I'm putting together a team of high quality, powerful men into self-improvement making online more than $10K per month to kickstart a civilizational project, at the beginning a nomadic tribe of men into self-improvement off the grid, soverign. But to get into that place they need first to overcome The Trial. The initiation ritual. A 3 months journey by sea from England to Karelia, northen Russia, where we'll hunt wolves and each men will have to undergo a series of tests to prove he can become part of our brotherhood.

Journal about it. Be truthful and honest with yourself. Don't cope.In the end the pain of regret is always going to be stronger than the pain of meeting resistance with relentless effort and trying with everything you have.We, as men deeply in love with GREATNESS, are blessed and cursed with awareness. Awareness of our true potential. Piotr Rzepecki.author: Piotr Rzepecki
Piotr Rzepecki.text:
Most people focus on the wrong things → content, followers, looking professional.
But none of that guarantees money.
Attention ≠ income. System = income.
Right now, most people operate like this:
Post → get views → maybe engagement → no sales
And then they think the problem is reach, algorithm, or niche.
It’s not.
The problem is there’s no structure behind what they’re doing. No path. No sequence. No flow.
Just random actions that don’t connect.
Here’s the shift you need to understand: Most money is NOT made on the first sale
Beginners think: I need one perfect product, I need one perfect offer
Reality is different.
First sale = entry point
Some people even go: Break even or lose money on the first offer → just to get the customer in
Why would anyone do that?
Because of this:
1 purchase → much higher chance of 2nd purchase
And that’s where the real money starts.
Not from convincing strangers once. But from serving people who already trust you.
So instead of thinking: How do I sell this one thing?
You need to think: How do I build a path someone can follow?
A simple structure looks like this:
Attention → content, posts, videos
Capture → something valuable in exchange for contact
Trust → consistency, follow-up, useful content
Offer → clear, relevant solution
More value → upsells, deeper or faster outcomes
That’s the system.
Another important reality:
~7 interactions → before someone buys
So if you post once and expect results → wrong expectationIf you post a few times and quit → you never even gave it a chance
People need to see you multiple times. They need to understand you. They need to trust you.
Trust is built through repetition.
That’s why follow-up matters so much.
Not aggressive selling. Not spamming links.
But showing up consistently, giving value, explaining things clearly, and slowly removing doubts.
Now here’s where most people lose again:
They finally get one sale → and stop there
That’s one of the biggest mistakes.
Because:
Best moment to sell again = right after someone buys
At that moment, trust is highest Resistance is lowest.
That’s why this structure works:
Main offer → upsell → downsell → additional value
Important rule:
Main product must already deliver value on its own.
Upsells are not there to fix it. They are there to enhance it.
Example:
Basic solution → advanced version Slow result → faster result Simple info → deeper system
All optional, all relevant.
Now what if someone says no?
Upsell X → Downsell
Lower price, Simpler version, Less friction
And many people who said no at first → say yes here
Because it feels like a better deal
This is not guesswork. This is based on how people actually buy.
Now zoom out again.
Everything starts with a simple entry point:
Value → attention → relationship
You give something useful. You attract the right people. You start building trust.
You’re no longer chasing random traffic.
You’re building a base:
Audience → trust → repeat buyers
That’s how long-term money is created.
Not from one post. Not from one product.
But from a system that keeps working.
Another mistake people make:
They build once → and never test
They assume everything works.
But small issues break everything:
Broken links, confusing offers, bad flow.
And every mistake = lost money
And you don’t get those people back
That’s why people who get results:
Test everything. Go through the process themselves. Fix problems early Improve step by step.
Because once traffic starts coming in, You need the system to work.
Now compare both approaches:
No system: Post → hope → no results
System: Attention → Trust → Offer → More value
One is random. One is controlled.
And control is everything.
Because when you control the system, You control the outcome.
You know what’s working. You know what’s not. You know what to fix.
And that’s when things start to scale.
So if you feel stuck:
It’s not your content. It’s not your niche. It’s not your potential.
It’s the lack of structure behind what you’re doing.
You don’t need more ideas.
You need a system that turns attention into results.
Build it once. Improve it over time.
And everything changes.

Piotr Rzepecki.author: Scott Northwolf
Piotr Rzepecki.text:
I'd expand on this:
"A simple structure looks like this:
Attention → content, posts, videos
Capture → something valuable in exchange for contact
Trust → consistency, follow-up, useful content
Offer → clear, relevant solution
More value → upsells, deeper or faster outcomes
That’s the system."
In the following way:
To actually draw attention consistently instead of posting into the void and hoping it will work or spending a lot of time honing your skills to guess what people like... I personally do this:
Select a niche (a group of people without stating their "problem", at this point I don't really know what their most painful percieved problem is).
Conduct niche research interviews (minimum from 14 to 30 but the more the better, but do not procrastinate by elongating this stage) using a script with questions to figure out what's their biggest percieved problem and their desired outcome (you'll pick up on the niche's language as well, this is important).
Build high ticekt offer with the knowledge that you now possess (you should have picked up on the most recurrent patterns) [you can use Alex Hormozi's framework from his book $100M Offers, that's a very good one for getting started].
Develop the methodology on the back-end of your offer in order to help people attain their desired outcome, if it feels like you don't even know if you'll be able to do it that's good, it means you'll have to grow and learn to be able to deliver consistently.
After selecting the niche properly and finding where they hang out and approaching them to get them on a niche research interview... guess what? You already have the skeleton of your outbound system. You just need to go back to the same place and ask them to jump on a call with you, if possible also gather them in one place like a skool community, an instagram page or an email list (this is where content for nurturing your audience becomes important, not saying that starting with content is a bad approach but this is just how I do it and it works for me).
Create sales' script (test for 30 reiterations minimum to know what are your conversion rates with a big enough data sample and when you make a change test always for 30 reiterations) [Jordan Belfort's The Way of Wolf is an amazing book for getting started creating your own sales' script].
Run structured sales' calls consistently and record them in order to optimize.
Track your KPIs (ABR, SUR, GFR and SCR).
Reverse engineer the numbers to your desired monthly income (I think I'll make a post about that soon).
Ok, so then to summerize:
Select niche.
Run niche research interviews (14 to 30).
Grand slam offer.
Methodology development.
Appointment setting (outbound system).
Sales' script development.
Record and optimize sales' calls.
Track your KPIs.
Reverse engineer your desired monthly income periodically using the KPIs data.

Piotr Rzepecki.author: Piotr Rzepecki
Piotr Rzepecki.text:
When you’re on the field, everyone has a different role.
Someone defends. Someone creates chances. Someone scores.
But the game only works when people play together.
It reminded me a lot of communities.
A good community isn’t just one person posting.
It’s people sharing ideas, helping each other, and moving things forward together.
Just like in football, the real value appears when everyone is involved ⚽

Piotr Rzepecki.author: Scott Northwolf
Piotr Rzepecki.text: Also in war. It's the same on all mortal games of cooperation.

Piotr Rzepecki.author: Piotr Rzepecki
Piotr Rzepecki.text: Most people don’t fail from lack of knowledge. They fail from lack of action.  Facts: 👉 70-90% of people never finish courses. 👉 Most never launch anything. 👉 First attempt is usually bad, but necessary.  What actually works: 1. Launch fast First version in 24-72h  2. Talk to people Send 20 messages Goal is 3-5 real responses  3. Post daily Even simple posts build momentum  4. Improve based on feedback Not on your own ideas  5. Repeat Every week, not once  That’s it No results = no volume More action = more feedback = better results Start now Fix later

Danielis Dudaitis.author: Lead
Danielis Dudaitis.text:
Gotcha good to know. You are right, many people struggle with their attention span.

So basically, first i can say that i am 15 years old. Maybe a little shocking for someone. I have always felt more mature than everyone else around me. Now many people ask, why so early? My whole life i have hated the idea of school and the system itself. Sure, if you want to be a doctor, you have to study. But in my case i really want to achieve freedom and live by this quote; Do something you love, and you will never work again.

So i got on self improvement maybe 1 year ago, but it has been lots of ups and downs. 1 month you are so dedicated and then the other you completely forget that you even are on this thing.

Alright enough of that ill tell you more about me now.

My name is Danielis, i live in Norway and i want to create one of the biggest AI companies in the world.

7-8 months ago my life took a big turn. I was just beginning to do basketball coaching, i landed my first client at only 14 years old. No big deal but 47$ a month. And then i got struck with a prolapse in my spine. I couldnt walk, play basketball, jump, run, go out with friends. NOTHING!! It was the worst month of my life (october 2024).

Lucky for me, i found my girlfriend at a halloween party and she pretty much saved me. Whilst i had nothing to do, she would come to me and spend time with me. The basketball coaching basically died that month. I still talk to my client, but no payments taken from him yet.

Now im much better. The prolapse is getting better everyday but it is still hurting like a bitch. Its hurting while i write this actually. So it has been rought but, lately i watched this movie (50 shades), and after watching it ive gotten this sudden thurst for money and for achieving financial freedom. I know that i will make it one day, so why not start now. I have tried many things but now im going into learning AAA. Only problem is getting a laptop so that i can start working. Still gotta wait on that. So as a hobby/job ive opened up a barbershop. I make around 300$ dollars a month from working with my own hands.

So soon ill get my laptop and start the long process.

Thank you for letting me share this Scott. I hope it was understandable.

Danielis Dudaitis.author: Scott
Danielis Dudaitis.text: I appreciate you sharing your story, bro. The reason why you have forgotten at times about self-improvement is because you need to build systems to stick to the good habits and keep growing consistently, that's why deep journaling (to figure out what's your goal) and a timetable (to reverse engineer those goals into a system, daily habits and daily consistent action towards them) are going to be your two best friends on this journey. At the beginning setting the goal will feel daunting, but you actually need to start backwards for it to work, so you start by building a timetable, a set of habits, a routine, you just think... what are the best habits I can have in my life that are the highest ROI I could possible conjure for myself? It's simple! Working out for the body (with your issue that might no be an option, but maybe light swimming, walking or something like stretching, cold plundges, sauna and breathwork can help wiht your recovery, focus also on high quality nutrition for fast-track recovery, read Deep Nutrition by Catherine Shanahan and start watching Oscar Patel and start following the LPO method, you'll be surprise to discover that the issues you are experimenting come from bad nutrition, and it's normal, everyone is eating the wrong way these days, the "normal food" is actually posion and you need to gain awareness of that as soon as possible and start taking responsability for every single thing in your life, absolute personal responsability will give you boundless power, because responsability is directly proportional to power, remember that, it changed my life and I'm certain it will change yours as well as soon as you embrace it), read consistently to gain knowledge and keep growing, every single day, start small if you need to, don't let ego blind you, if you are only capable of reading two pages per day or per reading session that's fine, don't force it, organic growth is the best, stay on course and last but not least meditate for expanding your consciousness, now it might sound kind of weird but trust me, this is one of the most powerful things you can do for yourself and will turn you into an absolute beast. We can go into more depth about it but this is a quick overview of it all. Take action, don't just sit there, read this words and move on to the next thing. I want you to right now pick up your phone, go to the note's section, make a new folder called routine, organize the notes to be sorted by the date of creation and create your first timetable with today's date, paint a picture there of your perfect day, the most productive day of all, including those habits I just mentioned, have a score to track how many you did, gamify the thing and to each score of amount of tasks completed asign an animal, the higher the score the most prestigious the animal. I'll show you some examples but don't wait for it, do it now, build it with the hours that you'll do each task, when you wake up and when you go to bed, once you start building it it will start feeling natural and you'll think of all the things you want to include. Take a screnshot of it and send it to me. The world rewards the men who take action, not the ones who think, moan and complaint. I believe in you, brother, you can do this. You can achieve whathever you set your mind to, start building discipline now to turn dreams into a reality with consistency. Come one! I want to see that timetable!

Eduardo Daly.author: Lead
Eduardo Daly.text:
I´ve been thinking lately about what you told me when instead of practising game I should become the real deal, which is true ngl.

Still I would like to know your story and what it means to become the "real deal", and how can i apply it to my context, because I´m not interested in business right now, I want to lvl up my social skills, which is why I´m practising game, but for me to present real value to women and be more dominant I need to have wins backing up my arrogancy.

I have a great physique and great reputation in Uni as a smart guy, I would also say I´m quite attractive compared to my competition here in the Netherlands.

Thx

Eduardo Daly.author: Scott
Eduardo Daly.text: I admire you capacity to reflect and lean towards growth. The growth mindset vs the fixed mindset. I'm sure you've heard about it before, it's a popular concept on self-improvement.

Eduardo Daly.author: Scott
Eduardo Daly.text: Even though I got a way with words I'm a man of action and I like pragmatism so let's jump on a call, 8 PM Bali time (in almost 7 hours from now), here's the link: [https://calendar.app.google/sXGUvR5vTqJXS4sF7](https://calendar.app.google/sXGUvR5vTqJXS4sF7)

Eduardo Daly.author: Scott
Eduardo Daly.text: I'll see you in an hour and a half, brother.

Eduardo Daly.author: Scott
Eduardo Daly.text: Any questions let me know, brother and as soon as I have some time available I'll get back to you.

Eduardo Daly.author: Scott
Eduardo Daly.text: Leveling up body, mind and soul is a life-long journey and trust me when I tell you that the depth of each field is truly daunting but the thousand miles journey begings with one single step. So start walking, brother. I believe in you... you've got so much potential. I want to see it become an actuality.

Eduardo Daly.author: Scott
Eduardo Daly.text: No man can help another without helping himself. This is my way to give back to the world all the blessings I have recieved.

Charlie Jamieson.author: Participant 1
Charlie Jamieson.text:
Hey bro, thanks for your advice! Just a couple things:
- I'm 14
- I am trying to make it pro in football
- I don't have a bank account
- I have no clue how to start

Charlie Jamieson.author: Participant 1
Charlie Jamieson.text: I already have a YouTube channel with 400 subscribers and a solid history

Charlie Jamieson.author: Participant 2
Charlie Jamieson.text: To become a pro athlete the younger you get started the better, bro. How do you plan to make money if you don't have a bank account? My advice would be to go to your parents first and be straight and absolutly sincere about it, try to explain them what do you want to do and how do you expect to be making money and that you do not require for their investment or anything, you literally just need a bank account and a payment processing method (so basically a bank account attatched to a debit card [of course NOT a credit card, a debit one] and something like Paypal or Stripe to process payments [if they can open up both for you even better] connected to such bank account) and if they can facilitate you with that you take care of everything else (but don't worry, even if they say no we can work around it, it's just the easiest way, let's work smart).

Charlie Jamieson.author: Participant 2
Charlie Jamieson.text: And I mean, since you are not asking them for any money, you're just asking them for a chance to start making your own money with your passion, is not just really wholsome but quite free of risk, even in their view, because usually parents are concerned about their kids getting scammed if they go out and about with money and a bank account and all but if you got literally an empty bank account for yourself the worst thing that could happen is that by some freak accident you actually make money and lose it somehow, which is not a problem because you made it anyways, and the thing is if you can make money once you definetly can do it twice. Also, we both know you won't make money by accident, this is very VERY intentional.

Charlie Jamieson.author: Participant 2
Charlie Jamieson.text: Keep me updated, man, let me know how that goes. If it goes south we can figure out other options, so don't worry about it, do not let anything discourage you, stay strong, stay relentless.

Charlie Jamieson.author: Participant 2
Charlie Jamieson.text: If there's anything you need just ask, bro.

Charlie Jamieson.author: Participant 1
Charlie Jamieson.text: Appreciate this bro, next step for me is the brainstorming 👍😁

Zaya Sambuu.author: Scott
Zaya Sambuu.text: Hey, Zaya, your story really stroke a chord. Thank you so much for sharing.

Zaya Sambuu.author: Scott
Zaya Sambuu.text: What kind of business are you into?

Zaya Sambuu.author: Lead
Zaya Sambuu.text: Thank you, Scott. I really appreciate you taking the time to read and reach out.

Zaya Sambuu.author: Lead
Zaya Sambuu.text: At the moment, I’m not running a business. I work as an auditor at a small firm.

Zaya Sambuu.author: Scott
Zaya Sambuu.text: Oh, I see. And what do you do as an auditor there? Also, are you planning on starting your own business? If so... do you have anything in mind?

Zaya Sambuu.author: Scott
Zaya Sambuu.text: By the way, the fact that you are from Mongolia is just soooooooooooooooooo cool. I just had to mention it. Even though I've heard the city is kind of polluted but the landscape and the countriside I've been told it's nothing but pure bliss, mountains and open plains and pure fresh air, a profound sense of expansion for the soul. Would you agree with that?

Zaya Sambuu.author: Lead
Zaya Sambuu.text: I’m not running a business at the moment. I’m exploring the Paramount Tax franchise in the U.S., and I previously worked at the Mongolian Tax Authority for 12 years.

Zaya Sambuu.author: Lead
Zaya Sambuu.text:
Yes, what you’ve heard about Mongolia is true. Our country has truly beautiful nature — it’s a very special place. You should definitely visit Mongolia someday.
It’s winter here now, and during the day it’s around –35°C. But even this cold has its own kind of beauty 😁

Zaya Sambuu.author: Scott
Zaya Sambuu.text: I love the cold. And I love history too, and Mongolia has such a rich history! It's truly a contry that I have always wanted to go. I have read so much about Genghis Khan and his exploits, the Golden Horde, how they got into the very heart of Europe inviding Viena itself! It's sooooo epic! I love it!

Zaya Sambuu.author: Lead
Zaya Sambuu.text: Yes, Mongolia has a very rich history and culture. Our nomadic heritage and connection to nature are still very alive today. I think you’d enjoy experiencing it in person someday.

Zaya Sambuu.author: Lead
Zaya Sambuu.text: By the way, where are you based?

Zaya Sambuu.author: Scott
Zaya Sambuu.text: Your people are still very connected to their nomadic roots and that's something I profoundly admire. Old European aristocratic values steem from a nomadic worldview that was passed on to them from the Indo-Europeans from Southern Russia and modern day Ukraine and the profound impact that has had on the whole Western world and its development is truly amazing to study and understand in depth.

Zaya Sambuu.author: Scott
Zaya Sambuu.text: I'm living in Bali, Indonesia.

Zaya Sambuu.author: Lead
Zaya Sambuu.text: Wow, Bali! That sounds wonderful. I’ve heard great things about the energy and lifestyle there.

Zaya Sambuu.author: Scott
Zaya Sambuu.text: Everything you might have heard still falls short to the WONDER this beautiful place is. People here are truly THE MOST open, loving, kind, interesting and ambitious people I have met in my life. I feel truly at home here, like I never felt ANYWHERE in the whole world before, not even back home where I come from.

Zaya Sambuu.author: Lead
Zaya Sambuu.text:
Yes, I’m Mongolian, and I’m very proud of our history, our culture, and our beautiful nature.
Recently, I also realized that being Mongolian has shaped me into a very resilient person 😁

Zaya Sambuu.author: Lead
Zaya Sambuu.text:
I’ve never been to Southeast Asia before, and I never really thought about going there.
But I’ve heard from many people that Bali has a very special and beautiful energy.
That’s why I’ve really started wanting to visit Bali. I hope to go there someday soon and experience what you’re feeling for myself.

Zaya Sambuu.author: Lead
Zaya Sambuu.text: It was really nice talking to you. It’s getting late here, so I’m going to get some rest. We can always continue the conversation another time.

Zaya Sambuu.author: Scott
Zaya Sambuu.text: If you want to come I'd be glad to show you around. I have actually met already here people from Mongolia and I think you'll find it truly inspiring. And you should definetely be VERY proud of your country and culture, it is truly AMAZING! It's been truly great meeting you, Zaya. I'm looking forward to our next chat and I'd love to see you introducing yourself in my community, Self-Improvement Nation. It's great to have you with us.

Zaya Sambuu.author: Lead
Zaya Sambuu.text: Thank you so much, Scott. I really appreciate your kind words and the warm welcome.I’m glad to be part of the community and will introduce myself there. It was great meeting you as well, and I look forward to chatting again another time.

Zaya Sambuu.author: Scott
Zaya Sambuu.text: Community call coming up: [https://www.skool.com/self-improvement-nation-3104/inauguration-community-call](https://www.skool.com/self-improvement-nation-3104/inauguration-community-call)

Zaya Sambuu.author: Scott
Zaya Sambuu.text: Hey, Zaya, how's it going?

Zaya Sambuu.author: Lead
Zaya Sambuu.text: Hey Scott! Everything is going great, thank you for asking. How about you?

Zaya Sambuu.author: Scott
Zaya Sambuu.text: I couldn't be better! I was wondering what have you been up to lately? Have you made any porgress towards establishing your own Paramount Tax franchise in the U.S or have you changed your mind with regards to that?

Danyal Jafar.author: Lead
Danyal Jafar.text: Hey, you commented on my post about the women, what did you have to say over DM? i'm interested in hearing

Danyal Jafar.text: Instead of practicing game to get women... woudn't be smarter to ACTUALLY become the man who women lust over? It's just thinkign from first principles and I tried both pathways and I can tell you... there's no point of comparisson between pretending to be the man and faking confidence, learning game and tricks with actually BECOMING HIM.

One thing is I don't make eye contact with girls too often, maybe because I'm a bit short even though I've worked on my appearance a lot. If I did I'd probably do more approaching. In university right not focusing on learning german, securing a good internship by 2027 and landing a high-paying job once out of university, will start a youtube channel In February.

Danyal Jafar.author: Scott
Danyal Jafar.text: You're still going to be approaching women first most of the time, but they'll be actually inviting instead of defensive...

Danyal Jafar.author: Scott

Danyal Jafar.author: Lead

Danyal Jafar.author: Scott
Danyal Jafar.text: I don't have a lot of time now but schedule a call with me and I'll give you as much value as possible in one hour call as I can. I'm not a dating coach or anything like that because most daiting coaches are the gayst amongst all to be honest, I work with self-improvement coaches here in Bali so just to be clear I don't have anything to sell you since you are definetely not on my niche but I want to help you out regardless, no man can help another without helping himself and I love to help my brothers whenever I got the time. [https://calendly.com/northwolfscott/recruit](https://calendly.com/northwolfscott/recruit)

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: I'm shooting you a DM so I don't forget to connect later on, Kerry. Like I said, I'd love to hear your story of how you got into coaching, I'm very passionate about self-improvement and social skills to be honest.

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Hey Scott. I have always been into self development. I read more non fiction books about personal growth than anything else, written 2 myself. Trained as a life coach two years ago but as you know it's never ending. Growth doesn't have an end point.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: Exactly! That's wonderful to hear, Kerry. I'm also wondering... what got you into self-improvement?

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: My desire to know myself.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: One of the 3 maxims written on the Temple of Delos in Ancient Greece was "Know Theyself". And they had a very good reason for it...

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Exactly.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: How long have you been doing coaching for? Like... officially? Is it something you are doing right now? Like your main focus?

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Officially a year. Paid clients since October. Only one and it's starting to grate on me if I'm honest. I am excellent coach. Lots of testimonials. Lots of respect. Only one paying client so far.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: That's great, Kerry! One step at a time.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: Are you doing something else besides the coaching at the moment?

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Written my 2nd book. It's at the publishers now. Been accepted, currently under review.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: That's soooo cool! What is it about? I love non-fiction as well. I guess all of us into self-improvement read mostly about that...

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: This is my 2nd book. First was autobiographical. This one actually is fictional. About the power of friendship. It is about connection.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: Interesting... are you into spirituality by any chance?

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Yes

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: You just got WAY cooler.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: When you speak about a fiction book that reflects a certain teaching about friendship and the power of connection for some reason makes me think about something along the lines of The Alchemist.

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: I love that book.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: Someone very especial I met in person not very long ago (she's on my community as well) asked me a very interesting question. She said that she realized that most people remember certain specific parts of the Alchemist as the most impactful for them and that everyone had a different part. Mine was the line that "Everything is just ONE THING." For her it was about the secret to having a good life contained in the story of the young man that goes to ask this question to a very wealthy king and he sends him to go around his city holding a spoon with oil in it, instructed not to drop it. Do you remember that story?

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: I read it a long time ago. You are making me want to reread it!

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Right now!

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: I think I'll reread it soon because I was impressed by how hard it was to me to remember that part of the book and how profoundly impactful is the teaching contained in it.

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Is the teaching about purpose?

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: Basically the king sends the young man to go around his kingdom and make sure he doesn not drop the oil from the spoon, telling him to come back in an hour because at the moment he was too busy.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: When the guy comes back the king asks him if he saw all the wonders and beauties of his kingdom, the gardens, the palace, the exotic animals, the markets. They kid answers he did not. The king then tells him to go back and this time take a look at all of that but still without dropping the oil from the spoon, because he's still busy for another hour or so.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: The kid goes and sees all the wonders of the kingdom and is impressed by the beauty of the city. Then the king asks "Where's the oil in the spoon?" and the young man realizes he has dropped it distracted with all the things he was seeing. Then the king tells him "The secret to a great life is to be able to appreciate all the beauties of the world without ever losing focus on the oil in the spoon.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: That realization sends shivers through my spine. It's the very same principle of meditation itself. To get lost in thought only to bring back your focus to your anchor gently, without judgment. The simplicity but insane profoundity of it is truly astonishing to me...

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Thank you for writing all that out for me. I am extremely interested in 'focus' at the moment.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: Thank you for your attention. This is a topic that I'm truly passionate about.

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: You are speaking my language. That commands my attention.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: Kerry, you're really cool. I could swear I already mentioned that but it requires some emphasis on it. I'm actually planning to start recording some cool podcasts on these subjects. Would you be down to have a conversation with me about this as a podcast thing? I think it'd be really interesting...

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Yes absolutely!

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: That's awesome! Let's schedule it in, then, this is my calendly: [https://calendly.com/northwolfscott/meeting](https://calendly.com/northwolfscott/meeting)

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: I was talking to a friend that has been running his podcast show for some years now and he was telling me I should go back to make some cool podcasts out of the amazing conversations I'm having so frequently but not really leveraging, and I thought "You know what? You're right!" so I'd be really glad to have you as my first guest to my new podcast show...

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: It's called Answer 42.

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: It's scheduled. So our conversation tomorrow will become the Podcast?

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: Yeah! Exactly!

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: It's 4 PM, Bali time, for me... I have a meeting right after that, do you mind if we make it one hour earlier, Kerry? I don't want us to have the conversation in a rush.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: If it doesn't work for you now problem, we can do it at that time as well, it's just to make sure we have time to spare...

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Sure.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: Awesome!

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: Let me reshcedule it and send you the link.

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Thank you. 😀

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: [https://calendar.app.google/o1CWKxQNFFqdmWxy8](https://calendar.app.google/o1CWKxQNFFqdmWxy8)

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: there you go...

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: it should have landed on your email as well just in case.

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: It's gonna be epic!

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: I'm looking forward to it...

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Looking forward to it too. Especially because this the 2nd day in a row I have been told the answer is 42...

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: There are no coincidences in this world, Kerry, only synchronicities... the key is to be aware of them. Thank you so much for your invaluable presence. Tomorrow we'll meet.

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Looking forward to it Scott. ☺️

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: Ready for our call in 19 minutes?

Kerry Souter.author: Lead
Kerry Souter.text: Yes absolutely!

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: Awesome! See you then...

Kerry Souter.author: Lead

Kerry Souter.author: Scott
Kerry Souter.text: Thank you for joining me, Kerry. It was an absolute pleasure.

Kerry Souter.author: Lead

Here’s the link to our podcast episode together ([https://youtu.be/y2BW6tBvUeQ?si=Mt4lQGPAGL40jvOE](https://youtu.be/y2BW6tBvUeQ?si=Mt4lQGPAGL40jvOE)). Ethan Shahan.author: Ethan Shahan
