Ethan Shahan.author: Scott Northwolf
Ethan Shahan.text: @Ethan Shahan it's just a matter of understanding how your mind works and how dopamine (the neurotransmitter in charge of motivation) works.You can quite literally manipulate your own mind and "trick" it into viewing the work as the most fun thing in the world, you just have to take away from your life ALL the other instant gratification activities and basically bore yourself to death and then... the most fun thing in the world is to do what you are supposed to do.The scientific explanation of this is a little bit more lenghty but I'm guessing you get the point.If you starve yourself for an entire week a salad will look like an exquisite meal to you. *disclaimer: this is not nutritional advice.

Ryan Grant.author: Lea Newkirk
Ryan Grant.text: @Ryan Grant I like your take on this, especially the quote. Have you always felt this way or is it something you learnt along the way?

Ryan Grant.author: Ryan Grant
Ryan Grant.text:
(edited)
@Lea Newkirk Something I learnt along the way for sure.
When I first started to explore self improvement I genuinely thought if I was doing my meditations, gratitude journaling etc... everyday I would become successful 🤣
I didn't realise that they help you do the things you need to do to become successful to a higher standard. It's all indirect. Which sounds crazy but yeah that's what I used to think lol
But yes, action is the needle mover of everything

Ryan Grant.author: Lea Newkirk
Ryan Grant.text: @Ryan Grant I know you are laughing but I am going to be honest that is exactly what I thought once upon a time too 😂 the thing about that is it is actually a need to build the foundation because we aren't really taught to sit with our shit and write it down so it is fully valid and I am proud of you for doing that for yourself! I really needed this reminder today so thank you! So what is your action for the day?🥹

Ryan Grant.author: Scott Northwolf
Ryan Grant.text:
@Ryan Grant holy shit! Mike Mentzer is my father's favorite bodybuilder. That's the OG, the actual Bodybuilder Philosopher. He was very deep into Ayn Rand's objetivism which I love.
The fact that you mention this makes me wonder... are you doing Heavy Duty?

Ryan Grant.author: Ryan Grant
Ryan Grant.text:
@Lea Newkirk haha thanks! It's part of the journey I guess. When I realised that meditation etc... are indirect tools that will help me do the things I need to do better, that's when I started to see glimpses of progress :)
At the moment I am going to get back to comments in here and then edit some YouTube videos. What about yourself?

Ryan Grant.author: Ryan Grant
Ryan Grant.text:
@Scott Northwolf Indeed, I love all of his stuff. I am not fully doing Heavy Duty however I've iterated it slightly with a more heavy influence of Dorian Yates. I read his book a while back and it was super insightful I think I am going to re-read soon haha.
Are you into that sort of training too?

Ryan Grant.author: Lea Newkirk
Ryan Grant.text:
@Ryan Grant Love that take on meditation! even more than that, I LOVE that you're doing youtube, let's hop into the DMs because I have so many questions about this.
Action for today, sanity walk on the treadmill.

Ryan Grant.author: Scott Northwolf
Ryan Grant.text: @Ryan Grant have you read Muscles' in Minutes by Mike Mentzer? That one blew my mind...

Ryan Grant.author: Ryan Grant
Ryan Grant.text:
As other synthesizers have pointed out in recent posts...
Making sure that the work we are doing is productive and in alignment with our goals is SO IMPORTANT!
And as people say with fitness & the majority of other things too...
What you do outside the gym is just as important as inside the gym.
Regarding this context:
what you do outside of work can be just as important as you working, because the things you do outside of work can either make or break your next work day.
With that being said...
how do you prime/prep yourself for a KILLER work day?
How do you make sure you are 100% and ready to go?
Is that through any planning/organisation strategies?
Going to the gym? Going on a walk?
Any supplements?
Any meditation/practices?
What's your go to priming routine/practices for a killer work day right now? 👇

Ryan Grant.author: Scott Northwolf
Ryan Grant.text: Wake up, read my self transcendence document, meditate for 1 hour, do gratitude journaling, read for 1 hour, workout (usually for 1 hour), jump into my pool for 20 minutes or so and get some extra sunlinght, growding and sprints, shower and then sit down to do deep work.

Ryan Grant.author: Scott
Ryan Grant.text: Broooooo, Dorian Yates changed the bodybuilding world alltogether when he came into the scene... I mean... he started the whole era of the massive physics.

One of those two is infinite and you can always make more... the other one is limited and you can never get it back. Dustin Jenkins.author: Scott
Dustin Jenkins.text:
This is what I do for young men:

# Stage 1:

First we lay the foundation, I teach them how to set goals through deep journaling. You can't get where you want to go if you don't know for starters where you want to go. Through deep journaling you also teach yourself to think in an orderly, structured and logical manner and can develop actionable steps to achieve your goals, basically reverse engineer such goals into daily habits and tasks. That's where the timetable comes in. I teach them how to build their timetables to get consistent at the habits that will take them from where they are to where they want to go.

# Stage 2:

Usually we all want a strong aesthetic body and be healthy, have energy and vitality, so I teach them how to get consistent at the gym and the secrets of nutrition (must people are clueless about nutrition, for example most people think vegetables are healthy but vegetables are filled with anti-nutrients like oxalates and phytic acid, oxalates bind to iron, zing and magnesium in your blood and render them useless for you body to assimilate, that's why they are called anti-nutrients).

We all want wisdom and knowledge to make sound decisions and to know which path to take, so I teach them to get consistent and reading which is the most profound way to acquire knowledge there is.

We all want to have good mental health, control over out attention and a personal understanding of the structure of reality, that's why I teach them how to meditate and eleveate their vibrational frequency. How to do gratitude journaling consistently and to expand their consciousness.

# Stage 3:

And last but no least we need a purpose, a way, a path, a mission. I teach them how to find it and follow it. Build their online businesses, help other people (potentially as educational content creators) and once they are ready and if I see they are worthy I take them into my inner circle and get them ready for the next stage.

# Stage 4:

I'm putting together a team of high quality, powerful men into self-improvement making online more than $10K per month to kickstart a civilizational project, at the beginning a nomadic tribe of men into self-improvement off the grid, soverign. But to get into that place they need first to overcome The Trial. The initiation ritual. A 3 months journey by sea from England to Karelia, northen Russia, where we'll hunt wolves and each men will have to undergo a series of tests to prove he can become part of our brotherhood.

Abdallah Ll.author: Scott
Abdallah Ll.text:
Deliberate practice:
1.Define: what is the problem (what do the pins on the lock look like)
2.Hypothesis: Come up with a hypothesis to solve it.
3.Act: Try it straight away to either succeed at solving it or fail as quickly as possible and acquire critical information.

Reflecion Upon Your Practice:
4.Analysis: Reflect upon if the thesis that you acted out was correct and accurate at solving the problem. Did it solved it? Fantastic! It didn’t? Can you identify what’s wrong and reiterate on the next time you act?
5.Hypothesis reconstruction: Come up with a different hypothesis based on the little information collected from your practice.
6.Second reiteration: Try again and keep repeating this loop until you obtain the desired results.
7.Data collection: From the insights that you’ve got and the critical information that you have gathered start building a body of knowledge that shall guide you in case you forget or that can guide and help others to obtain the same results.

Theoretical Knowledge applied to your practice:
8.Seek: actively searching for the required information in the places it can be found
9.Receive: your sensory system coming into contact with the information
10.Process: your brain processing the information
11.Judge: judging whether the information is true or not (is it objectively accurate?)
12.Decide and Act upon it (test out the 3rd hypothesis born out of somebody else’s insights and/or body’s of knowledge): conscious decision to act on the information, deciding what to do about it and implement.

Hey Scott! Welcome to Evergreen Evolution🌲 We're excited to support you on your journey.
To get started, we offer a personalized onboarding call. This is a great chance for us to tailor your experience and explore the resources that will benefit you the most. Reply 'Link' and I will send it!

Coach Dave

David Ladin Jr.author: Scott
David Ladin Jr.text: Link

David Ladin Jr.author: Scott
David Ladin Jr.text: Hey, David, thanks! I know this must be an automated message but I'm really looking forward to see what I can learn from this community and the wonderful people I'm looking forward to meet and connect with.

David Ladin Jr.author: Lead
David Ladin Jr.text: of course it is automated 😉

David Ladin Jr.author: Lead
David Ladin Jr.text: its the absolute best way we can ensure I get to greet you personally

David Ladin Jr.author: Lead
David Ladin Jr.text: We are excited you are here

David Ladin Jr.author: Lead
David Ladin Jr.text: How did you find us?

David Ladin Jr.author: Scott
David Ladin Jr.text: I was looking for self-improvement communitites here on skool, I enjoy a lot meeting cool people into meditation, spirituality, working out, building their online businesses, learning about themselves and the world, reading consistently... You tend to get pretty lonely when you get into self-improvement, it's really hard to relate to people anymore.

David Ladin Jr.author: Lead
David Ladin Jr.text: 100% AGREE

David Ladin Jr.author: Lead
David Ladin Jr.text: that’s the reason we created Evergreen. There is no magic potion. We’re all on the same journey once we make the choice, just at different points on our path. Welcome.

David Ladin Jr.author: Scott
David Ladin Jr.text: exactly!

David Ladin Jr.author: Lead
David Ladin Jr.text: You’ll find I will quote “The Road Not Taken” by Robert Frost and “The Alchemist”. If you’re unfamiliar with either please tell me. I’ll share a link to either.

J'son Elizondo.author: Nick Banik
J'son Elizondo.text: Hey everyone, my name is Nick Banik, and I'm a certified men's life coach and Neuro-Linguistic Programming (NLP) Master. I am an exited founder who built a digital agency and got it acquired and then I became a day trading coach. I studied NLP throughout that and got my certifications and started working with ambitious men in life coaching who were on a similar journey that I went through. I basically wanted to create a way to work with men that I wish I had when I went through all the experiences that I did.I've been coaching for a while across disciplines, but more of the life coaching and life purpose coaching with NLP is more recent over the past several months after getting certified. I'm currently acquiring clients in this capacity for 1 on 1, and it's going well. I seem to be attaching entrepreneurs, which makes sense because that's my background.I'm happy to be here, fam!

J'son Elizondo.author: Des Cooke
J'son Elizondo.text:
I have a close contact who is a content writer, a ghost writer essentially. One of the things the ghostwriting community has been saying for a very long time is that AI will never replace them because of the fact that you can witness ai content, as you say you can see it.
One of the recent developments is that that person has now got an AI machine essentially that's taken time to build.
That machine is trained on lots of a person's content, like:
it's trained on their videos
it's trained on their podcasts
it's trained on their blog posts, etc.
They can literally talk into their phone about a story, an idea, a thought, a meeting. "I've just had a conversation with the director about blah blah blah blah blah, and by the time they get back to the desk, there's a post already written that takes almost no editing!
It is now spitting out posts that are relevant to the arena the person works in, providing it's trained enough and that the input posts are actually good enough.
Someone like me has decades of back catalogue.
That ghost writer is moving f̲r̲o̲m̲ ghost writing,
with the community of people in ghost writing saying "AI will never replace them",
t̲o̲ delivering people access to this server that can be trained on their back catalogue so that it can replace their posts within the content that they actually have.
I suppose this is a bit like using Notebook LM, etc., but this seems to be much more advanced, and I've seen some of the output.
Initially, he was completely distraught and downhearted that he'd built himself out of a job, but this would actually be a product with the hundreds of files put together and the person accessing it remotely linking their own content for it to be trained on. Essentially, a cost to set it up, and be trained how to use it and train it - then the customer could run it on their own machine.
It's actually as good as he can do now as a ghostwriter and is indistinguishable from his own written content, even by someone like him who notices the normal AI slop a lot.
As Ferris Bueller said: "𝘓𝘪𝘧𝘦 𝘮𝘰𝘷𝘦𝘴 𝘱𝘳𝘦𝘵𝘵𝘺 𝘧𝘢𝘴𝘵. 𝘐𝘧 𝘺𝘰𝘶 𝘥𝘰𝘯'𝘵 𝘴𝘵𝘰𝘱 𝘢𝘯𝘥 𝘭𝘰𝘰𝘬 𝘢𝘳𝘰𝘶𝘯𝘥 𝘰𝘯𝘤𝘦 𝘪𝘯 𝘢 𝘸𝘩𝘪𝘭𝘦, 𝘺𝘰𝘶 𝘤𝘰𝘶𝘭𝘥 𝘮𝘪𝘴𝘴 𝘪𝘵."
It'll be a matter of weeks, possibly months, before the world has caught up with him. And you won't be able to tell?
Question: Did I write this with AI?

Wait a minute... actually competition is good because it sharpes our skills and it pushes us to be better...
I must retract on what I said... this is a catastrophe!

J'son Elizondo.author: Ailandas Sulcas
J'son Elizondo.text:
I totally agree.
We start using tools that make life easier without realising that easier doesn't necessarily mean better. AI is a good example of that.
We use it to lighten the load on our brain but then we lose our ability to be analytical, logical, and intellectual.
It's good as a tool but it's bad as anything else.

I tried to rewrite a few songs I wrote long ago with ai (fews days ago) Fortunately the results were so bad that I decided to go on my own again 😅 @J'son Elizondo you are a wise man- thanks for you words!

J'son Elizondo.author: J'son Elizondo
J'son Elizondo.text:
𝗔𝗶 𝗦𝗹𝗼𝗽.. 𝗧𝗵𝗲 𝗗𝗲𝗮𝗱 𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗿𝗻𝗲𝘁 𝘁𝗵𝗲𝗼𝗿𝘆 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗦𝘆𝗻𝘁𝗵𝗲𝘀𝗶𝘇𝗲𝗿 𝗣𝗼𝘀𝘁𝘀..
Yeah I said it Synthesizer is being filled with articles written by BOTS! 🤖
Funniest thing I've seen is 2 articles back to back written by two separate people, same message almost the exact wording. (Not funny at all actually)
The information in this place used to be 𝗱𝗲𝗻𝘀𝗲, 𝗿𝗲𝗮𝗹 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝗿𝗮𝘄..
More value per syllable than any other community..
But now its being littered with em dashes, regurgitated nonsense and oh yea my favorite one, intellectual parroting (sarcasm).
The dead internet theory is a concept predicting that the internet will become predominantly composed of AI-generated content and bot activity, rather than human interaction..
The sad part is that WE are spreading this ai slop. (Literally doing the bid of AI).
By increasing the pace at which it swallows, LITERALLY engulfs the internet whole.
We are masquerading.
Devaluing our own authenticity.
Impersonating what it's like to be a human.
I want to share that community is built on connection and connection is real and raw.
And RAW makes mistakes. Those mistakes in the moment to you, might seem like flaws or blemishes that should be covered. But I want to argue that it is those blemishes are what will distinguish YOU from all of the other articles, posts and NOW 𝗔𝗜- 𝗚𝗘𝗡𝗘𝗥𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗 𝗦𝗟𝗢𝗣 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗚𝗥𝗔𝗠𝗦.. <-- (Can you believe that ?!)
⭐Studies now show that we even speak like ai ⭐
Tell me if you've heard this.
"Not only is this not x, But its Y"
"Its not x but Y"
And words like DELVE 🤣😆😂
The Rule of Threes. 𝗘𝘅. ( Clear, concise, and compelling) (I DONT even feel right using the rule of threes in formulating my writing because I think people will perceive it as AI. (Even though I speak like that naturally as a philosophy reader)
Its naturally rolling off of our tongues at this point because we consume it SO OFTEN.
ONLY increasing the very PROBLEM that this community aims to solve in the first place..
🌊"𝗪𝗲 𝗮𝗿𝗲 𝗱𝗿𝗼𝘄𝗻𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗶𝗻 𝗶𝗻𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗺𝗮𝘁𝗶𝗼𝗻, 𝗯𝘂𝘁 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘃𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝘄𝗶𝘀𝗱𝗼𝗺"🌊
With every article generated by ai you are effectively adding MORE water to the already filled bucket.
I want to share with you that not polishing an article, post or comments is actually going to 𝗵𝗲𝗹𝗽 𝘆𝗼𝘂, 𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝗵𝗶𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗿 𝘆𝗼𝘂.
🤮Articles on social media migration and social media rebellion states: "THEY ARE LEAVING BECAUSE IT NO LONGER 𝗙𝗘𝗘𝗟𝗦 𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟" SAYING IT 𝗙𝗘𝗘𝗟𝗦 𝗔𝗥𝗧𝗜𝗙𝗜𝗖𝗜𝗔𝗟, 𝗨𝗡𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟 𝗔𝗡𝗗 𝗠𝗔𝗡𝗜𝗣𝗨𝗟𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗.. "IT FEELS LIKE A 𝗙𝗔𝗞𝗘 𝗘𝗡𝗩𝗜𝗢𝗥𝗡𝗠𝗘𝗡𝗧 𝗖𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗘𝗗 𝗕𝗬 𝗔 𝗠𝗔𝗖𝗛𝗜𝗡𝗘" ..🤮
So with all that in mind..
Think before you use ai.
Think before you let it write your articles for you.
And for god sakes don't let it write your whole program from conception to birth, so you can COLD pitch it to someone FOR money.
𝗧𝗛𝗔𝗧 𝗶𝘀 𝗻𝗼𝘁 𝗯𝗲𝗮𝘂𝘁𝗶𝗳𝘂𝗹 𝗺𝗼𝗻𝗲𝘆. 𝗧𝗵𝗮𝘁 𝗶𝘀 𝘀𝗰𝗮𝗺.
A NOT so clever scam that aims to take advantage of people who are in pain and need experts, 𝗥𝗘𝗔𝗟 𝗦𝗬𝗡𝗧𝗛𝗘𝗦𝗜𝗭𝗘𝗥𝗦, 𝗡𝗢𝗧 𝗔𝗜 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗺𝗽𝘁 𝗽𝗿𝗼𝗳𝗲𝘀𝘀𝗶𝗼𝗻𝗮𝗹𝘀.
Authenticity over curation
Authenticity over curation
say it with me now
Authenticity over curation

Can we alliviate pain without authenticity?
Yes.
Can we create trust without authenticity?
Maybe... but it's difficult.
A personal brand is memorable.
Authenticity allows you to create that personal brand and it's in general... well, more enjoyable I'd say.
What were we talking about?
Never mind!
Let's focus on becoming USEFUL.
That's meaning!

J'son Elizondo.author: Scott Northwolf
J'son Elizondo.text:
Bro, I agree completely. I am a writter at heart and you and I have this beautiful obsession with knowledge and understanding, and I know you draw as much satisfaction from teaching and sharing with others your knowledge as I do... and it is quite literally like a drug for us... but in a good sense, because we get to connect very deeply with amazing people and we get to touch their lives in very impactful ways changing it for the better.
I'm really glad you are bringing this to people's attention because this is something I ALWAYS say to people: If you let AI write for you... I mean... can you really take pride on what "you wrote"? It's not even your own knowledge... and it's sad... because by teaching we solidify our knowledge.... by teaching we learn at the same time.
This is what we are here for and letting something as beautiful as that die would be a true shame.
At the same time... I don't think the AI slop is so bad. It's actually WONDERFUL! Because the more of it there is out there the more we will stand out for being authentically ourseleves and the more rare we'll be. We'll be the only ones willing to do the "hard work" of writting and teaching ourselves and we will benefit greatly personally from that.
The work works more on you than you work on it, and that's a fact, so when people chose the easy path, the path of least resistance they are missing out on the amazing gift that focus and deep work bring to their lives, the development of their discipline, the development of their capacity to actually sit in presence on the present moment and give their best to something beautiful and meaningful like it is connecting with people and helping them change their lives.
I truly loved this post. Thank you so much for sharing it with me, brother... and above all: thank you for being you, Jason, you are a shinning lighthouse in the middle of this dark sea and you have no idea of how deeply appreciated you are, man.
Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion, but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brother! Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

J'son Elizondo.text:
Yoooo, Jason, this is an extract from a deep journaling session I had yesterday, man, I got to be straightforward with you, brother:

“The sad reality is that I’m already struggling to stay consistent with my appointment setting… since that’s the case… focusing at the moment ALSO on trying to build this lead magnet for my skool community shows the level of arrogance and stupidity in which I’m unconsciously engaging. The lead magnet is a great idea, but I must be humble enough to draw the line on the limits of my capacity for focus and consistent action. And this is putting way too much on my plate, more than I can handle at the moment. I must be able to first and foremost stay consistent hitting my current minimum KPIs, land my first hight ticket client and then and only then add more to start scaling. I can’t do this right now. And it hurts so badly my ego and my sense of commitment to Jason because he already placed effort and time on helping me out… but this does not mean I am not going to implement it and use the resources he gave me… this only means that it will take one or two more months than I had anticipated (as usual I always end up being overly optimistic about how much work I can handle and how fast I can get certain things done… I must be more aware of this and start saying “no” more often… as a matter of fact… from now on I ABSOLUTELY MUST say NO to EVERYTHING and EVERYONE if I want to protect my energy and focus and accomplish this massive goal. One thing at a time. I must not allow myself to get distracted with shiny objects and try different new things when things get hard. I must finish what I started first before moving on to anything else. I know this! Why do I keep forgetting these fundamental principles for success? This is why I’m struggling so much, because of my own inability to follow my own advice. Impulse control. Focus! Focus! FOCUS!).
The plan is clear and set. No more distractions or deviations.
30 qualified sales’ calls with the current sales’ script (v1.3). I got already around… let me check… 6! 24 more to go. Unqualified and without using the script I have more, like 9 more if I remember correctly but those do jot count on my book.
Fine…
Let’s get this shit done!”

Now, with that said this is the structure I came up with for now for the community:

So this will be basically the roadmap for new members who enter my community:

Self-Improvement Nation Roadmap:

1-Introduce yourself to the gang here (…).
2-Book an onboarding call with me (it’s free, don’t sweat about it) so I can blow your mind with the coolest conversation you’ll ever have about self-improvement, consciousness and the structure of reality itself and so we can get to know each other.
3-Get introduced to the ‘Reverse Engineered $10K Method’ for self-improvement coaches here (…).
Bonus!!!
You get weekly community calls to hang out with the gang, answer cool questions to get to know each other better and keep each other accountable with regards to our goals and on top of that you’ll get the opportunity to ask me any questions you might have about the roadmap or my methodology. I’m happy to answer them all!

/////// /////// /////// /////// /////// /////// ///////

The Reverse Engineered $10K Method

1- “What to do?”
You can check out my posts in my community.
2-“How to do it?” I can give you my time on a consultation call for which of course I got to charge, because I don’t have infinite time and I value it highly as you surely will value the powerful information I’ll be sharing with you.
3-“What, How, When and Where?” In other words… if you want me to implement all this critical information with you, keep you accountable and take in the responsibility that you do hit the $10K per month in 42 days with me, implementing my full system, the ‘Reverse Engineering $10K Method’, then we can work together through my program and we can commit fully to this life-changing transformation, which is by far the best way I got to ensure the highest probability that you actually get there.

/////// /////// /////// /////// /////// /////// ///////

So I don’t think it’s going to be productive to have our call this very same week. Maybe next one will be a better idea to touch base on the progress on my side.

But if you have something you need to ask me or need my advice on we can either jump on that call tomorrow still or schedule a new one for this weekend and talk about that.

Tell me your thoughts, bro.

Sandra Nat.text: @Sandra Nat it’s a matter of progressive overload… over time you get better and better, but you must begin setting the bar to success low enough so you can hit it consistently every day and start leaning just beyond your edge…

Sandra Nat.author: Sandra Nat
Sandra Nat.text: delusion backed up just by words is just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action - Can't agree more. Nobody's going to move the needle for you. It's up to you if you take actions and move forward.

Sandra Nat.author: Sandra Nat
Sandra Nat.text: bad habit? probably sleeping late at night and waking up late in the morning. i guess i'm not an early bird, but would love to wake up earlier and have more time in the day to work and live :)

Sandra Nat.author: Scott Northwolf
Sandra Nat.text: @Sandra Nat deep down in the back of our minds we already know what we must do... it's all about making the commitment to do it where the true change resides...

Sandra Nat.author: Scott
Sandra Nat.text: Community call coming up, Sandra. Hope to see you there. It's gonna be epic: [https://www.skool.com/self-improvement-nation-3104/inauguration-community-call](https://www.skool.com/self-improvement-nation-3104/inauguration-community-call)

Sean Mullarkey.author: Lead
Sean Mullarkey.text: Does it work 5 PM Bali time for you tomorrow? Here’s the link: Sean Mullarkey's call with Scott Northwolf

Sean Mullarkey.author: Lead
Sean Mullarkey.text: Actually, I just realized I can’t share the link on skool’s app from my phone, I’ll share it again when I’m back home through my laptop, bro.

Sean Mullarkey.author: Scott
Sean Mullarkey.text: What’s the call gonna be about?

Sean Mullarkey.author: Lead
Sean Mullarkey.text: I want you to tell me your story so far on self-improvement, like if you were telling me the plot of a movie in which you are the main character.

Sean Mullarkey.author: Lead
Sean Mullarkey.text: Then your goals and aspirations moving forwards and if you have identified them what are your current struggles and main bottlenecks right now that are preventing you from movingly forward (if you have).

Sean Mullarkey.author: Lead
Sean Mullarkey.text: Then, if I see you posses a growth mindset and you are open to it I’ll share with you my advise and the actionable steps (in as much detail as time allows us) I’d take if I were you on your position right now in order to get what you want. If you are honest with yourself and your mentality is in the right place you’ll know that as a man what you want, like every single other men, is POWER (I’ll explain in more depth exactly what I mean by this).

Sean Mullarkey.author: Scott
Sean Mullarkey.text: Have you ever struggled with mental health?

Sean Mullarkey.author: Lead
Sean Mullarkey.text: I went through a 4 years long chronic depression, psychologists, psychiatrists, psychoanalysts (those ones were particularly expensive), pills. All for nothing… I just wanted to kill myself even more. I thought the solution was a girl, my dream girl, and I managed to get in a relationship with her… didn’t work either (we actually stayed together for 3 years but that’s another story)… in the end I started watching self-improvement content, first Jordan Peterson for like a year on the self-improvement depression phase but then I came across Hamza… built my first timetable, started following it with the three core self-improvement habits (working out for the body, reading for the mind and meditating and gratitude journaling for the soul)… after only two weeks I got out of my 4 years long chronic depression… you can imagine my surprise… it’s been a little more than a thousand days since that first timetable. I didn’t stopped there… now I live in Bali, have my dream physic, been traveling the world, get any girl I desire and have the attraction of women, I got my own online business… I have achieved my goals and dreams… now I’m setting new goals… bigger goals…

Sean Mullarkey.author: Lead
Sean Mullarkey.text: True greatness can only be born of true suffering. Pain is the catalyst for the growth of the masculine spirit. It will either break you or turn you into a force to be reckoned with.

Sean Mullarkey.author: Lead
Sean Mullarkey.text: Are you coming to our call in one and a half hours, bro?

Adam Avenus.author: Scott
Adam Avenus.text: Welcome to Self-Improvement Nation, brother! Glad to have you with us, Adam. Can't wait for tomorrow...

Joseph Difilippo.author: Scott
Joseph Difilippo.text:
Welcome to Self-Improvement Nation, Joseph.

Start by introducing yourself to the community here ([https://www.skool.com/self-improvement-nation-3104/say-hello-to-the-gang-start-here](https://www.skool.com/self-improvement-nation-3104/say-hello-to-the-gang-start-here)) .

I’m really glad to have you here with us, brother.

Looking forward seeing you in the community and I hope you can make it to our weekly community call, it’s every Saturday at 7 PM Bali time and it’s TRULY fucking EPIC!

Joseph Difilippo.author: Scott
Joseph Difilippo.text: Joseph, like I mentioned before, we are doing a weekly community call and that shit is EPIC, man. I really hope you can make it and get to hang out with the gang, it’s insanely cool, check out the calendar here and make sure to add the call to your calendar, mark it with a red X, set up an alarm, get a reminder, include it on your timetable, add it to your to-do list, tell your father, your mother, your siblings, your girlfriend your fucking dog about it! I’ll see you there, bro: [https://www.skool.com/self-improvement-nation-3104/calendar](https://www.skool.com/self-improvement-nation-3104/calendar)

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: Tell me, brother, I'm curious, how is it like to live in an ashram? I have always dreamt about it, actually I almost went to live in one in Spain as well, curiously. The one that the Tibetean monks have on Orgiva, near Granada.

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text:
beautiful man !

I'm near Barcelona in Pierra in Amma's ashram

life in an ashram is different depending on the Guru and the Ashram itself

the One I'm currently in has very strict discipline and heavy load of volunteering, we work in an olive farm daily regardless of the hot temperatures

the one I'm currently in pushed you to the edge of your performance in term od body and mind

But I've been I some that are more like 5 star hotels with jacuzzi and tennis courts

whatever the ashram one thing is clear you feel a sense of peace and home

what prevented you from living in one so far?

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: I guess it wasn't meant to be. At that moment I coudn't get my visa because of an issue with the institution that was supposed to provide the reason for the visa in the first place, it was a students visa and that institution didn't have the credentials of the autonomus community of Andalucia to do that, and by the time I was going to try to get another place my host family coudn't afford more money to pay for the other university, so we gave up on that after more than 9 months trying. I would have carried on but I can't force others to put themselves into a bad situation because of me. So I figure it was fine and I would just carry on with my life until my next opportunity.

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: After that I was planning to go to China and on the first opportunity I had I'd go to mount Wutai and join a taoist temple there. But my travel to China wasn't possible either because the plain tickets got too expensive and my budget back then shrunk, so I cound't afford it.

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: I ended up traveling to Malaysia and kickstarting my journeys as a digital nomad, making money online and experiencing life in the South Asian East 6 months after my 3 year long relationship ended with a girl I truly thought I'd marry and start a live with her. But it was truly for the best. The amount of growth I've been through since then has been truly amazing...

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: After Malaysia I traveled to Canggu, Bali in Indonesia, which is where I am right now. I am planning at some point to go to India and I might get to experience life in an ashram there, who knows?

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text:
wow sounds like a lot of things you couldn't predict happened and took you on wild ride
wel as long as you have the desire to get into this kind of lifestyle it will happen

I believe there are ashrams in bali too maybe going there for a day would give you an idea of what it is

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: definetely! I want to visit Ubutu, they say it's an incredibly spiritual place...

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text: Ubud you man?

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: I'm currently at Canggu. But I'm planning to visit Ubud.

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text:
Nice Ubud is something very special it’s a magical place that will magnify all of you

I truly recommend to go there and get nourishment from the energy of the jungle

How long are you staying in bali ?

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: I don’t know if I’ll ever leave… there’s something about this place… I had never felt like that. I had always been somewhere wishing being somewhere else. It doesn’t happen that here to me.

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text: I was thinking the same when I arrived then my path changed

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: Everything is temporary and eternal at the same time. The boundless present moment keeps expanding upon itself like an ocean...

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text: yeah I heard this theory

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text: do you have a spiritual mentor ?

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: No, I don't.

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text: Are you on a spiritual path ?

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: 4 years so far...

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text: great and what lessons have you learned so far?

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: What haven't I learned would be a better question. It changed my entire perspective on reality itself. I know now that we are God having a human experience, that everything is just ONE THING. That everything posseses a level of consciousness. I learned how to manifest, how to bend reality with the power of my will, how to set a goal and move energetically towards it. How to control and manage pain, how to overcome suffering... you are me, and I am you, and there is no separation. We are not a drop of water in the ocean... we are the ocean in a drop of water.

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text: this is beautiful, how dod you came to this realisation?

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: By living, brother... just learning to see reality as it is. It came a point at which my meditations would become so deep that I would start feeling like reality was only to be found on my meditations and the world we percieve through the senses was nothing but an illusion. You can interpret it that way if you want but to me it's not. The world that surrounds me is nothing but an extension of myself. By exploring it I'm knowimg myself. By interacting with the people I'm just talking with different projections of source consciousness, and I am source consciousness. Of course, not everyone can accept this because some are not ready for the revelation, and even by telling them they would not understand, deny it or simply ignore it and not really integrate such knowledge into their experience of reality, and it's fine, those are just fragments of source consciousness who are on their own path of evolution of their souls on their way back to source, even if they have to go down into density first. But everything is perfect and exactly how it

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: *how it's supposed to be...

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text: I happy to see that you have a dedication to learning and discovering yourself through the world, yet still wonder why you haven't found a mentor yet

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: A mentor will appear when I need him. I don't have to look for him.

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text: certainly :)

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text:
Hey brother!

I'm making a free workshop this Friday on how to harness the power of the mind, wanna come?

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: Infinite gratitude for your invitation, brother. I can't promise anything because I'm running on a very tight schedule right now, but please tell me the hour (including time-zone) and I'll do my best to be there.

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text: it's 8 pm CET, I don't want flaky answers, If you are 100% in i

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text: 'll send you a link, if not I'll wait for you to make it a priority

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: I´m sorry, brother, is not a priority to me right now. I won´t be attending for now but I really appreciate you.

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text: see that a better and more honest answer

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text:
Hey man how have you been?
What are you celebrating this week?

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: The full moon, of course! I did my water purification ritual at a temple in Ubud today. It was quite an experience!

Asha Hope.author: Scott
Asha Hope.text: And you? How have you been? How have you been celebrating the full moon?

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text: Oh nice I loved going to water temples I was always a unique thing and I love water

Asha Hope.author: Lead
Asha Hope.text:
haven't truly been doing full moon rituals since I left Bali

my life has become a ritual since I live in an Ashram

but what I've been celebrating recently are all the connections I've been doing

Justice Calabro.text:
Ayeee, blessings! This community literally changed my life in ways I could never have imagined. So I just wanted to give you a warm welcome Scott.

I'm Justice, the community manager, and lead performance coach in our Conscious Creator Community . Think of me like the New Earth Inspirationalist haha, my role is to really activate that creative fire in you and see yourself as the badass conscious creator & revolutionary you are!

Hopefully this community can become a truly nourishing space for you & support you in sharing your gifts with the world; it certainly has for me and so many others!

Blessings

Justice

Justice Calabro.author: Scott
Justice Calabro.text: Appreciate the automated message, brother, but make sure to fix the personalization, it shows member.name instead of the name and stuff, you know, those scripts sometimes get outdated, I really wouldn't know because I'm not sure of what you guys are using and I haven't really implemented anything like that on my community yet since we are only 66 members and it's still managable.

Justice Calabro.author: Lead
Justice Calabro.text:
Hey brother, sorry for the complications, it’s a brand new system. We’re testing out. Thank you for your understanding. ❤️‍🔥

It is a joy to have you in the mix nonetheless, and it is indeed beautiful that you are showing up and engaging. I look forward to seeing what magic you have to bring and again my apologies for the goofy message. ;)

We’re just experimenting making sure everyone gets the loving nudge that they deserve when they show up.

I would still be doing manual check-ins if it was possible with hundreds of people per day, but there’s a greater ways for me to show up and serve community. I just wanna make sure everyone still feels the love.

If yk what I mean ❤️

Thomas El Nadir.author: Miguel Ariza Lora
Thomas El Nadir.text: What's up gang,My name is Miguel, 25 years old and Spanish-Dutch currently living in Thailand.My journey in self-improvement began around 15 years old when I was overweight. I really wanted to be more like the others my age. Luckily this is when YouTube began to take off and people starting sharing a lot of value around fitness and nutrition. Trial and error got me to losing 20kg's eventually, and I've been able to keep most of it off ever since. When I saw putting some work in could give me results, I realized the same thing could be done in other aspects.The following years I became a lot more extroverted, social and confident. Through studying social work and psychology I got into coaching people to build a better life. I was good doing those jobs, and now I've taken it online and moved to Thailand. Felt like a good next step in my self-improvement. Have been coaching people now for 7 years already. Learned a whole lot. About people, training techniques, but probably even more about myself.I just think the self-improvement journey is amazing, and I wish everyone can experience seeing themselves improve into a better version and better life, step by step. Does it ever end? I don't think so. There's always room for improvement. It can feel tiring at times, but I wouldn't want to have it any other way.Can't wait to connect more with all of you guys.- Miguel

Thomas El Nadir.author: Scott Northwolf
Thomas El Nadir.text: @Thomas El Nadir brother, quite an epic story. What a journey! So many of us start motivated by one of the most basic desires we men have and that leads us to discover ourselves and start improving, some start with the gym, some start with game and social skills but in the end we all arrive at the same holistic understanding if we stay consistent on our paths for long enough.Really glad to have you here with us, brother. This is truly were you belong. And now it's time to build something truly wonderful with all the wisdom we have accumulated and give back to the world that has been so abundantly generous to us.

Thomas El Nadir.author: Thomas El Nadir
Thomas El Nadir.text: @Scott Northwolf Thanks man!Yes. Give, give, give and make the world a better place until receiving is just a natural consequence of living in this better place we created 🔥

Thomas El Nadir.author: Thomas El Nadir
Thomas El Nadir.text: Yo, Thomas here, soon-to-be 33 years old, French-Moroccan man currently living in Morocco.Can you briefly share from A to Z how you got into self-improvement?It all started at 17 when i wanted a girlfriend and googled "how to be more attractive to women" or something like that. I stumbled upon the "Game" community, the high achievers of the dating world, and oh boy it took me in quite the journey. This ultimately led me to understand how to attract and be charismatic not only from the outside but the inside, spirituality, and many epic adventures that got FAR beyond the dating world I initialy began with.What was the pain that got you into self-improvement?Loneliness and desire to connect (with women, at first).How did you get into self-improvement coaching/consulting?I went to more than a decade of life-changing experiences. Picked up many gems along the way that I realized with time would help so many people in the same struggle I was at 17 (and didn't go through what I went through to solve it).How long have you've been a self-improvement coach?It's been 7 years on-and-offAt what stage of your journey are you? (no shame if you're just getting started, we all got to start somewhere).Getting back to the coaching business with even more depth and background. And finally get rid of the shiny object syndrome that was such a curse of mine for that many years. Now that it's gone, i'm determined to build a powerful life for myself, and I have one more overcome to help people get out of to my arsenal ;)

Thomas El Nadir.author: Scott Northwolf
Thomas El Nadir.text: @Matija K thank you for sharing, brother! This is the very beginning of a TRULY FUCKING EPIC adventure if you're ready to put in the work and follow the roadmap.With Clarity, Commitment and Consistency sucess is guaranteed!You cannot fail, anyways unless you give up...

Love this, man. I also have the exact same tendancy to go on long proses and tangents when all could be said in shorter, more impactful ways.

We have a depth and thoughtfulness superpower.
With great power comes great responsibilities, and it is ours to make our wisdom accessible and actionable for those that can (and want to) benefit from it, in the way that hits home for them.

Flow Z.author: Scott
Flow Z.text:
I have perhaps too many recommendations when it comes to good transformative books. But depends on what you’re looking for right now… what area do you feel or know you should be growing yourself in? Need discipline, structure, a profound understanding of behavioral science and how to implement good habits that are conductive to high levels of performance? Start with Atomic Habits, then the 7 Habits of Highly Effective People to complement what is missing on the first one and Deep Work by Cal Newport to learn how to enter into a flow state and do the highest leverage tasks with the outmost efficiency in the shortest spans of time possible.

If you still need more on productivity and habits there’s a lot more but I don’t want to overflow you with way too many books because you’ll end up reading none of them and confused at the end. Better to have 3 or 4 good books and study them relentlessly to deeply understand them and apply the teachings contained in them.

But don’t wait until you have the information to apply. Raw action solves it all. While you learn theoretically still apply with whatever limited knowledge you might have at the moment and keep track of what you’re doing and the outcomes.

There are three components to the META-Skill:

1-Deliberate practice.
2-Reflection upon your practice (deep journaling is a very powerful tool here).
3-Theoretical knowledge applied to your practice.

To explain all this in more depth and detail and be able to deploy a proper action step plan or roadmap to follow to structure a powerful self-improvement/work routine we would have to jump on a call of 1 to 3 hours and I’d have to share with you a series of resources I used to share with my self-improvement coaching clients that… now that I’m thinking you could borrow some of that and use it with your clients as well adapting it to yourself if you see it fit. If that sounds like something you’d like to do that’s the way I see I could provide you with way much more value.
I charge for those kind of sessions €333 and even though I’m not really doing it anymore I believe you’d benefit greatly from it if you are serious about applying all this and moving radically the needle forward, brother.
Let me know how it goes and regardless of anything I hope this helps.

Laurits Valentin Offersen.author: Laurits Valentin Offersen
Laurits Valentin Offersen.text: I’m 18 years old and I’m fully aware that, on paper, I’m not that impressive yet.  I don’t have crazy money.I’m not a finished product.I’m still early.  But here’s the part most people won’t admit:  I’m delusional about where I’m going.  Not in an ego way, in a commitment way.I know I’m getting there because I’ve already decided I won’t stop.  That belief is what gets me up at 5 am  That belief is what keeps me working when no one’s watching.  That belief is what makes quitting impossible.  People call it arrogance.  I call it certainty.  If I didn’t believe that strongly in my future self, I’d move like everyone else, procrastinating, Hesitating and Half-committed.  But I don’t need proof yet.  I just need to stay consistent.  Being “delusional” is only a problem when you don’t work.When you do the work, it becomes inevitable.  if you do average ammounts of work you become average  if you do exceptional

Mark Santos.author: Mark Santos
Mark Santos.text: Hey Synthesizers!  During the fall of 2024 I uploaded 7 videos for 12 weeks straight and ended up going from 200 -> 800 subscribers.  I did this while being a 27 year old married man, working a full time job.  After hitting 800 subscribers I noticed three things: - I was unsure about what to sell - I didn’t have a single client - I only booked one call  I was confused about what to do next.  I hopped on Skool and was reminded about a beautiful thing that exists.  “The Synthesizer Roadmap”  I put my trust in Andrew Kirby and hopped the following 5 months on 100 calls - 65 networking calls - 30 discovery calls - 5 sales calls  As a result I closed 2 online clients on Synthesized deals.  That’s because The Roadmap allowed me to get on calls and discover real life problems.  This is where I could leverage my proof in multiple areas and create customized solutions.  How far are you into The Roadmap are you right no

Mark Santos.author: Scott Northwolf
Mark Santos.text: So this is basically what I have done:1-Picked a niche (people I really wanted to help and I identified with because I was them in the past, they were self-improvement coaches because that's what I was myself).2-Started conducting niche research interviews to figure out what was their problem and their desired outcome (didn't try to assume it, actually asked).3-With all the info gathered I build my offer and methodology ensuring good product to market fit.4- I developed a systematic sales' system with cold and warm outreach here on skool and jumping on sales' calls with the sales' script I developed.5-Calculated my Key Performance Indicators and reverse engineered them into daily outreach volume to hit my desired income.6- Recorded my calls and reiterated it in order to improve.7- After 3 months of no results but pure consistency I landed in one week my three first high ticket clients and surpassed $10K... (it took a while to get the sales' script and the correct outreach volume right)I think that sums it up...

Kasparas Stancikas.author: Kasparas Stancikas
Kasparas Stancikas.text:
Just before i tell you about the craziest 30 days of my life, i want to thank every single one of you for keeping me accountable under my daily accountability posts:)))
Thanks Synthesizers 🤝
THE STORY:
34 days ago my great friend told me:
"Kasparas, in a month there’s this amateur kickboxing competition."
2 seconds later, I said NO because right now i am:
Following synthesizer roadmap and thats my nr1 priority in life
i want to put all my focus into my business
Its too early I’ve only been training for 1 year
Excuses...
But as I continued with my day, this thought stuck in my head. At the end of the day i always had a dream to compete in atleast 1 martial arts fight
My gut intuition was getting stronger and stronger...
So I DMed my trainer to ask if I was even qualified to compete.
I got a response: “Only if you promise to train really damn hard.”
And that’s where it all started...
I quit bodybuilding and went all in on martial arts training 1-2 times a day and keeping myself accountable in synthesizer every single day for 30 days straight sharing how i am prepping for my fight + scaling my business all at the same time
There was one issue tho...
I still had no clue how would I get my parents to sign the agreement letting me fight? (i am 17yo)
My mom is really against fighting, she HATES it. My dad kind of does too. So, two weeks into training, when it was time to sign the papers, I did what any normal guy would do...
I faked the documents:D I forged my dad’s signature and everything.
My trainer had no clue, and it all worked out.
So 30 days later, after an insane amount of training and dieting (and still horrible cardio), I was about to step into the ring with another 81kg guy who was a year older and had 3 years of training to rip my head off
Round 1: Went really damn well. I was physically stronger, so I scared him away with some jabs and leg kicks.
Round 2: Still going decent, but I started getting extremely gassed out at the end.
Round 3: I had zero cardio left, luckily my opponent was hella gassed out too. This round was just to see who could survive the longest. And guess what? With only 10 seconds left, I landed a clear hook and KNOCKED HIM OUT, winning the god damn fight!
I still remember my friends screaming in the corner. (Watch the vid below.)
That was one of the BEST moments of my life...
I grew so much as a person, gained so much wisdom, and even managet to CLOSE MULTIPLE HIGH TICKET deals during the prep.
I proved to myself that ANYTHING is possible. If you really want it bad enough you can scale your business and chase your other wild dreams all at the same time
No excuses are valid.
I had to hide from my family, prepare for exams and run a business all at the same time...
if i can do it you can too
What is that dream you been pushing for later? (tell me in the comments)
0:26

Kasparas Stancikas.text:
@Kasparas Stancikas Different rulesets can be confusing sometimes, but it's the best way to gain more experience. My coach has me doing light kickboxing fights (not full power) before I do ring fights, so that I can gain experience with less risk.
However there are only 1-2 competitions like that per year in Portugal which is where I live. By competing in the Muay Thai competitions I have 4-5 competition opportunities per year, which is obviously much better👊

Ilia Kapustin.author: Scott
Ilia Kapustin.text:
“From the sideral space: the magical words”

I dreamt about a story untold
I dreamt about a glimpse of her soul
I dreamt and from my dream I didn’t want to wake up
But the dawn came
And that night that I saw her dancing
Committed to memory was sought
I sought her next morning but to find her my hands could not
She moved like the wind and such wind the last night of the year took off
I wonder if somewhere in the sideral space some magical words
Wait for me to find them and kindle the stone
The stone that to me seems her heart
The fearful heart of this girl that quenched my doubts and woke up
Woke up all these feelings I couldn’t express
I couldn’t feel
But sluggish and perhaps late now have dawned.

Ilia Kapustin.author: Scott

What are your favorite books?

Harry Montford.text: @Scott Northwolf ah yeah principles is a great book too I have to finish that one too, Transurfing has a lot of interesting ideas on pendulums, excess importance, and similar ideas like that I'm not sure if the metaphysical things are true but the book been helpful for me a lot

Harry Montford.author: Benedek Santa
Harry Montford.text: @Harry Montford Yeah, it's an interesting one. If you read the book "Psycho-Cybernetics", it's in a sense a similar book. Just maybe feels a little bit more of this metaphysical spiritual vibe to it. I'm personally not that much of a fan of this metaphysical, quantum physics, woo woo things, but personally, I find that there are certain principles that govern our worlds and we definitely see them. Maybe the author is wrong about what causes them, but he's definitely right about what's the solution to the problem. It definitely will make you see the world differently.

Harry Montford.author: Harry Montford
Harry Montford.text: @Scott Northwolf Great stack man! 100% agree with the points you made.

Harry Montford.author: Harry Montford
Harry Montford.text:
I've seen some supplements completely change people's productivity...
The main one I take is creatine daily. A lot of people take this for weight lifting but it also has GREAT impacts on your brain.
it replenishes ATP levels in our brain which is our main energy source.
This obviously improves your cognitive function but mainly during times you feel tired and low in energy.
So I wanted to ask, do you take any supplements to enhance your productivity?
If you have, did you notice any improvements in your productivity?
Looking forward to seeing answers from fellow synthesizers!

Harry Montford.author: Scott Northwolf
Harry Montford.text:
Vitamin D by getting sunlight in the morning.
Grounding to release ionic radiation accumulated in the body.
Sprints that boost your testosterone and growth hormone.
All possible vitamins consumed through a biodegradable source like animal organs.
No carbs, no poisonous foods. Avoiding Lectins, Phytic Acid and Oxalates.
You achieve more by removing what's holding you down and it's unnatural, not by adding weird shit made in a lab by skinny neck low testosterone bug-men.
Also meditation and full-blown "dopamine detox" is going to reseat your dopamine baseline and will allow you to focus and even have fun doing the "boring" work.
Funny enough the solution is boring yourself to death so you change your perspective and entering into a flow state because quite easy because there's quite literally nothing as fun as the work and the good habits in your day.
Reading is literally the highlight and reward of my day.
Everything else "feels" so hard at times that just sitting down and reading is actually super easy compared to the rest.

Harry Montford.author: Harry Montford
Harry Montford.text: @Dina Giugliano Of course! I know a few of my clients who took it and it didn't work that well. But then a few more found it insanely beneficial.

Harry Montford.author: Harry Montford
Harry Montford.text: We have 4 main deep work archetypes.  These are like the 4 classes you can pick inside of a video game. Each have their own benefits, buffs and weaknesses.  They are: - Monastic: 1 main task that you work on for most of the day, minimal breaks. - Bidomal: Long deep work days, but also complete rest days inside the week.  - Rhythmic: Deep work sessions in the morning, shallow work in the afternoon -> evening.  - Journalistic: No clear structure, one minute you could be partying in Bali, the next you are recording          a YT video.  My personal favourite is Rhythmic, but I also know many people who prefer the Monastic work type.  SO here's my question, what would do you follow and which one would you LIKE to try?

Harry Montford.author: Scott Northwolf
Harry Montford.text: Bro, I'm in Bali but I never do the Journalist style, lol 😂.For me is either Monastic or Rhythmic and I honestly prefer Monastic but it's truly an art and quite a skill to develop.

Devon Hoyt.author: Devon Hoyt
Devon Hoyt.text: What’s up everyone 👋 glad to be here.My name is Devon.1. From A to Z — how I got into self-improvement:Life kept handing me wake-up calls. Health scares, financial stress, family responsibilities, and realizing that nobody was coming to save me. I started learning out of necessity—how systems work, how habits work, how mindset works—because staying the same wasn’t an option anymore.2. The pain that pushed me:Feeling stuck while knowing I had people depending on me. That mix of frustration, fear, and responsibility is a powerful teacher. I didn’t want to just survive—I wanted to become someone my kids could look up to.3. Coaching / consulting path:Not a formal coach yet. I’m more in the “learn it, apply it, share it” phase. I help people by breaking things down simply, sharing what I’ve tested in real life, and being honest about what works and what doesn’t.4. How long I’ve been in self-improvement:Several years of trial, error, setbacks, and course corrections. Still learning every day.5. Current stage of my journey:Actively building. Still sharpening habits, tightening discipline, and leveling up consistency. Not finished—just committed.Looking forward to learning from everyone here and contributing where I can. 👊

Devon Hoyt.author: Scott Northwolf
Devon Hoyt.text: @Devon Hoyt epic, Devon! Thank you for sharing. Only when the pain of staying the same outweights the pain of facing the resistance and making a change is when we'll finally make a change. I heard from great men that the moment in which the truly became men was the very moment in which they realized nobody was coming to save them and they had to figure things out by themselves, it has been my experience as well.I'm really glad to have you here and looking forward to connect.

My story:
PRE 2023 -
Average young man, unhappy in life, had dreams of making money online, got nowhere, was motivated by Tate and etc, but could not take action, it was cause of bad mental health.
2023 -
Hit the lowest point of my life on Halloween 2023, decided to change my life as my eyes got messed up by a flashlight, I got eye strain could not distract myself any more I watched a full mental health guide from a YouTuber Hamza it changed my life, I tried to start business this year as well earlier, but failed cause of poor mental health.
2024 -
Started a business called "Copy Compose." Grinded hard for months,didn't realise that I made no success cause I got no joy or happiness from my work I made some decent money online that year but still was unhappy.
2025 -
I am now making money online with my passion and purpose of mental health, and I truly feel happy, fulfilled even beyond money, my mission is to help other entrepreneurs who have money but still ain't happy or fulfilled or want more money, and the biggest reason there like that is unhealed trauma.

My goal is $10K/month and gain freedom.  For those ahead on the path what’s the biggest lesson that accelerated your growth?34Last comment Feb 19

Bruno Manuel.author: Scott Northwolf
Bruno Manuel.text: Welcome to the gang, Bruno. Glad to have you with us, brother!One of the biggest things for me was when I stopped thinking about what I wanted and started thinking in terms of what other people wanted and needed.Choosing a niche, going to them and actually asking them consistently and in a methodical way what were their dreams and hopes and what were their biggest problems and using that to build a truly attractive offer that they'd resonated with and they actually wanted to buy and then coming back and offering that to them.And if you picked right the niche and you were able to find them to conduct niche research interviews with them... you'll be able to find them again on the same place and get them on sales' calls with you.Then all that remains is to conduct appointment setting, sales' calls, build a good script and funnel structure and track your KPIs (Key Performance Indicators) in order to improve over time, stay consistent and don't switch niches or business models all together because that resets your progress back to 0.

Alfie Lawrence.text: This is simple, brother, if we want freedom... like REAL fucking freedom and real adventure, a true brotherhood of Western men to have our own real nation... it all starts by gathering enough resources to have location and financial freedom. Most of us are gathering here in Bali. What we do is we work together to get them to $10K per month, they move here, we train, we gather at content creator's houses we organize on private villas, on the second phase we focus on applying the scientific method to our businesses to grow them faster, so what we are planning is to build a full blown monopoly over the self-improvement market. This is basically how you apply the scientific method to business: You break down a business into it's smallest components, like for example the sales' script, the appointment setting script (these two are the smallest components of a bigger component which is the sales' system). When you start a business (if you actually build it properly with the system that we use and you make something funcionable that is systematic and repeatable, meaning that you are not going to find magically clients one month and spend 3 others praying and hoping to see what happens) you would have version 1.0 of each bigger component comformed by version 1 of each smallest component. For example: Sales' System version 1.0 is built with sales' script version 1 and appointment setting script and methodology version 1. If you got sales' script version 2 and appointment setting script version 1 still you'd have Sales' System version 1.1. You get the point... the thing is that to actually know what's working and not you have to have a way to track metrics like SCR (sales' conversion rates), GFR (good fit rate) and ABR (appointment booking ration) amongst other things... all of this is actually a little bit more complex than this and more expansive but like I said, this is high level stuff for the second phase. Where I'm trying to get with this is that basically to actually make progress in this way you need to test different variables, the sales' script version 2 for example at least 30 reiterations (let's say 30 sales' calls) to actually know if it drives your conversion rates or not... if it doesn't you go back to the previous version, if it does you keep it. Some companies (the most successful ones) do this... but as you might have imagined it takes a really long fucking time. Now, imagine 100 companies built with the same exact model on one market (the self-improvement market) but with different niches... now imagine all of these companies actually are part of a group... imagine there's a Matrix of knowledge in the middle of all these companies collecting the data from their "A B testing" and redistributing it across all companies right away... this means that they can make progress quite literally 100 times faster. And this is what I'm building with my guys here to beat Alex Hormozi, make so much money that we can buy Putin's fucking army if we want and actually make all this stuff happen (starting with the ship, because that's just cool as fuck).

It works for a week when you're sick and tired of your situation, it works for a little while when you get some little nugget of inspiration whatching some Hamza video or Andrew Tate just talking about how good the life of a disciplined man is... but what you need it's A TRULY COMPELLING WHY. Like Nietzsche said "A man can bear almost any HOW as long as he has a WHY." You need something beyond discipline and something even beyond motivation. You need a WHY. A true powerful reason and until you've got that you will keep relapsing because you have built in your mind an identity attatched to these bad habits.

Efe Efe.author: Lead

Efe Efe.author: Scott
Efe Efe.text: Fuck mental health, fuck all the limiting beliefs and pre-convieved ideas you might have in your mind. For you to actually learn the very first step is to UN-learn everything you think you know. You cannot fill a cup that is overflowing and if your life isn't working... trust me the thing that is overflowing with it's not water... it's either tained or straight up poison.

Efe Efe.author: Scott

Efe Efe.author: Scott

Efe Efe.author: Scott
Efe Efe.text: Listen, it's not that you have anything wrong that cannot be fixed, it's that you haven't accepted a simple yet powerful truth that will set you free and will start to liberate you from all your shackles.

Efe Efe.author: Scott
Efe Efe.text: This simple truth is that YOU and YOU along are COMPLETELY AND ABSOLUTELY RESPONSIBLE for your LIFE and everything that happens within it.

Efe Efe.author: Scott
Efe Efe.text: Responsability it's directly proportional to POWER. The very second you blame ANYTHING besides yourself for your experience of life it's the very second you are giving to that person, thing, place or whatever it is all your POWER away. Because now you believe that this bad experience of life that you are having cannot be solved.

Efe Efe.author: Scott

Efe Efe.author: Scott
Efe Efe.text: So what's the cure to depression? Knowledge.

Efe Efe.author: Scott
Efe Efe.text: What's the cure to anxiety? A firm decision.

Efe Efe.author: Scott
Efe Efe.text: If you are able to accept this and understand it, to integrate it I can teach you the actionable steps that will build you up and will get you out of ANY and EVERY situation that you might percieved as bad in your life. Trust me, but if you cannot start by accepting ABSOLUTE PERSONAL RESPONSABILITY for your life there's truly nothing ANYBODY in the entire world can do for you.

Efe Efe.author: Scott
Efe Efe.text: I'm not helping you. I'm teaching you how to help yourself.

Efe Efe.author: Scott

Efe Efe.author: Scott

Paul Hedron.author: Paul Hedron
Paul Hedron.text: The basic components of a team are as follows:  A group of individuals, a vision, and a facilitator to direct effort and attention.  A leader must be an expert in the nature of the vision, the individuals on the team, and the conditions in which the vision and team interact.  Without a vision or goal, the collection of individuals cannot coalesce. Without individuals to coalesce, a vision or goal is without potential and impetus. Without understanding of the conditions, a team will experience unnecessary inertia, spending effort to achieve the vision.  A vision is merely a blueprint, something to build. The individuals on the team are the builders. The leader is the architect and facilitator.  The base component of a team and shared vision is the individual.  By understanding the individual, a leader has better command of complexity.  An individual has a capable skillset, and limitations. Understanding both the capability/capacity,

Paul Hedron.author: Scott Northwolf
Paul Hedron.text: When you talk about turning individuals into leaders, do you mean creating something like individual autonomous cells, like a decentralized organization?The thing is, I believe this can be either interpreted as giving enough understanding and power to each individual so they develop themselves and become truly useful members for the tribe OR as pseudo-anarchic kind of organization, a decentralized thing, kind of like the internet phenomenon of Anonymous.With the first interpretation, building upwards members of the tribe and getting them to a place of taking absolute personal responsibility for their lives, being capable of autonomously and sustainably grow and have their own systems in place for their development and for yielding consistently results for themselves and for moving forwards the cause of the tribe… that’s powerful and aligns with my vision and naturally will always align with the vision of any self-improvement mentor.But decentralized structures and multiple cells of command only work for very specific kinds of purposes and environments.I don’t believe we have the need to over-think all this at the moment.Things like culture, organization and logistics and politics tend to be better put into practice by empirical testing and exploration, especially at the beginning stages.

If not, that is fine, I can make others. Eventually, through tedious work and process, I will land upon a design that you find valuable -and- functional.

Otherwise, a template is merely a template. A construct of thoughts. Nothing more. Nothing important. I can, and will, make others, per specifications of individuals, and their own, each unique goals.

If this template is not working, or appealing, there are other ideas to explore. It either builds a kingdom, or it does not. This is fine. We merely move onto the next template, fit for you, and fit for your soldiers, vassals, advisors, and allies. There will need to be other kings. You merely need to be the king, of the kings. Lesser pests are things we don't need to tolerate, unless beneficial. Personally, I'd rather them all eaten by some duck or goose, or a goat is fine. A garden needs guardians. I don't care for the form, merely gardens, in essence.

If there are no gardens available at this time; this is fine. This is good data. We simply don't need to waste our efforts in making gardens of petri dishes.

Hey man. Your calendly says you're open in about 2 hours. You maybe open to a catch up call? Not sure how busy you are these days. Not really sure I'm doing alright. Something is bothering me, not exactly sure what.

I have a business template, I'm a step away from releasing it to the public. All this time I've spent building it, I neglected to pay attention to whether or not I should.

I turn into a monster with money. Money is easy. I need meaning. With all of the potential and possibility at one's fingertips, it's just not enough.

I've waited too long, thinking meaning would eventually materialize. Now, I'm in financial ruin, and I can't wait any longer.

I fear I am at true rock bottom; the foundation of financial freedom, nowhere to build but up, and nothing meaningful to build, nowhere to go. A nihilistic nightmare.

No fear to keep me in check, no drive to push me in any particular moral direction. Malaise. Frivolity. No respect, no regard.

Poverty has kept me safe. Its constraint is no longer.

Money is a mirror I don't enjoy looking into. I don't enjoy what I see in it, in myself.

Consider this Masonic. Brother to brother. I am destitute, and deprived of all minerals and metals, I am at the length of my cabletow.

Excellent man, that gives me more time to try to clarify my thoughts. I feel trapped, in some sense; I don't think a lot of people have really "lived the dream", so to speak. I can't really discuss money matters, with people that have never had money, or ... haven't really ever taken life seriously, have never lived, and generally don't live life seriously.

The "dream life" awaits me. It's simply not my dream. I don't want the money, or the dream life of others. I want my own. I simply don't have one, nor the time, anymore, to find one. I fear the resentment and gluttonous nihilism I may find myself in.

It's easy to put aside desires and base impulses in a deprived state. I don't -know- what I'll do, when that is gone. If a man can't afford top shelf booze, or a woman, he goes without. I know what I am, when I can afford these things. In some sense, I've ruined the last 10 years of my life, having made the mistake with financial fortune of the past.

I can't find the willpower to make more money again, knowing where it leads me.

This is perhaps my one last remaining puzzle piece I have, out of place. I have maybe 10 days, maybe, before I must place it.

I'm not sure you'll exactly understand quite fully, but you'll understand significantly more than most. It may be useful for you to learn these things, anyhow, in your future endeavors, as any young man on their way up. If not you, I'm sure you'll encounter others, it is meaningful mentorship matters and opportunity, nonetheless.

The dream of money is a nightmare in an instant, if not careful. The only thing worse than being a loser with no money, is a loser with money. A loser that has lost all, becomes a monster. Money can buy everything. I can't find anything worth buying.

Paul Hedron.author: Lead
Paul Hedron.text:
Hey man, I scheduled the call for 7, Friday the 20th. If you'd like, let's throw around ideas of what we'd like to achieve in the hour we have together.

Way I figure, it can go something like this:
-we figure out a general goal/outcome we'd like to arrive at by the end of the hour. This sets the stage for a stronger foundation for long term interactions.
-We consider possible best ways to start the meeting to determine the most efficient and effective trajectory and operational tempo to reach that goal.

From there, that establishes a preliminary template to re-use, if it's effective and efficient.

To support this, and to capitalize on the opportunity you're presenting; I'd like to gain insight into what you'd like to achieve and get out of our interactions.

From my end, here's what I'm looking for, and gain:
-You are remarkably similar to someone I used to know, someone I viewed as an older brother. You look just like him, interacting with you is like interacting with my brother, I'll likely never meet again.
-I enjoy learning and teaching. Anything we can share between us is meaningful, provided it grows and develops. I derive energy from this sort of thing.
-Time is non-linear, and compounds. The hour we have together is not -just- an hour: it's an hour, built upon every moment in existence leading up to it. When two individuals interact, that's one entire life, built by the entirety of existence up until that moment, multiplied by the life/existence of another. When interaction is directed and planned, refined information that otherwise takes lifetimes to achieve, is able to be shared much more efficiently and effectively. That information is then synthesized and integrated into two lives, and then can be tracked and developed further with additional interactions over time.

I've been busy contemplating our last interaction, as well as the meeting with Mr. Avenus. My mind works at different scales and speeds, I've fine-tuned the manner of non-linearity. It is my hope and intention that I will be able to have a meaningful update and demonstration of time dynamics in context to information sharing; this can be considered similarly to seeds. Think of it like this; share an idea with an individual, that idea develops and grows, using the lifeforce and mind of another, the seed develops autonomously. The seeds interact with other sides, in the environment of an individuals life, and it develops it's own ecosystem. Ideally, I'll have a meaningful report of where your ideas have gone, and what they have done, as well as a projected trajectory of where they will end up.

Please let me know any additional ideas you'd find fruitful to consider prior to our meeting. As benefit of non-linearity of time, mental simulation as a skill allows for rapid development outside of the limitations of linear solar time. The earlier an idea is entertained, the longer it has to mature and develop. This, in effect, as per nature of simulation, can allow for far quicker results than would otherwise be allowed for in physicality. This can be conceptualized as living out a lifetime in a minute, so to speak.

PS. I've been wondering about your previous project; getting a ship, forming a small crew of guys, and essentially starting a new culture, loosely/paraphrased. If there is anything I can do to assist with this, let me know. If you're trying to get people to show up to Bali, I can potentially help with this. I'd need details, clarity, and context, and I can begin forming a proposal of what I may be able to do to assist in this. My mind works quickly. I simply need details as seeds to cultivate. The clearer the details, and the quality of the details, determines the end outcomes. It can go very quickly.

Let me know.

Paul Hedron.author: Scott
Paul Hedron.text: I'll see you this Friday, brother. I appreciate a lot your words, man. With regards to the ship project everything is moving forward, I'm gathering more and more resources with that goal in mind and through my offer I'm developing precisely the kind of support network I need to bring that into life.
