Hey man, I wanted to get an idea of an itinerary to you prior to our call:

I'm attempting to make business more scientific, and potentially, more specifically, clinical. Measurable, repeatable. Observable, diagnostic, intervention, prevention. That sort of thing.

The intent is to have something actually usable and repeatable as a skillset. An actual science others can study and use. It looks like the field is less of a science, and more of an art, currently. Meaning, if it can be quantified and operationalized, it can be capitalized upon.

The tricky part seems to be acquisition. There seems to be no acquisition science at this time, that's what I've been mulling over. Acquisition science alone, would be a huge boon to individuals in self-employment.

To clarify; my interest lies in the science. Knowledge acquisition, accumulation, and transfer. The fiat transfer isn't a concern at the moment. Fiat is a representation. I'm only concerned with the actual field, at this time.

Two parents form offspring from their genes, modified by epigenetic expression in environments and experiences.

The offspring develops through environment and biology, laying the foundation for biological, psychological, and behavioral patterns.

This forms the foundation for personality development. (Personality)

The personality interacts with the environment and other personalities, in contexts and circumstances. (Events)

The pattern of interactions forms the archetypes; patterns of personality, interacting with other personalities, in contextual events. (Archetypes)

Archetypes interact with other archetypes in environments and in events, forming patterns of stories. (Stories)

Stories interact with other stories in environments, forming myths. (Myths)

Myths interact with other myths in environments, forming cultures. (Culture)

Cultures interact with other cultures, -shaping- environments. Shaped environments support cultivating cultures, which support or prune myths.

This modifies likely genes to propagate, and epigenetic expression.

The culmination of this process, is civilization. (Civilization)

Personality, times personality, is archetype. Personality Squared.
(Personality^2)

(Archetype^2 + Environment) x Events = Stories

(Stories^2 + Environment) x Events = Myths

(Myths^2 + Environment) x Events = Culture

(Culture^2 + Environment) x

Paul Hedron.text:
Meta-BioMyth Hypothesis in a nutshell:

Two parents form offspring from their genes, modified by epigenetic expression in environments and experiences.

The offspring develops through environment and biology, laying the foundation for biological, psychological, and behavioral patterns.

This forms the foundation for personality development. (Personality)

The personality interacts with the environment and other personalities, in contexts and circumstances. (Events)

The pattern of interactions forms the archetypes; patterns of personality, interacting with other personalities, in contextual events. (Archetypes)

Archetypes interact with other archetypes in environments and in events, forming patterns of stories. (Stories)

Stories interact with other stories in environments, forming myths. (Myths)

Myths interact with other myths in environments, forming cultures. (Culture)

Cultures interact with other cultures, -shaping- environments. Shaped environments support cultivating cultures, which support or prune myths.

This modifies likely genes to propagate, and epigenetic expression.

The culmination of this process, is civilization. (Civilization)

Personality, times personality, is archetype. Personality Squared.

(Personality^2)

(Archetype^2 + Environment) x Events = Stories

(Stories^2 + Environment) x Events = Myths

(Myths^2 + Environment) x Events = Culture

(Culture^2 + Environment) x Events = Civilization

(Civilization^2 x Events) + Environment = New Environments, new genes

(Genes + Environment)^2 + Events = Personality
-
At the micro and macro scale, long-term predictions can be made:

Tall genes in nutrient poor environments will stunt biological growth. The epigenetic presence of tall genes will be latent within the personality probabilistic expression, based on events and interactions with others. If genetically reproduced, offspring will maintain height genes, with epigenetic disposition of lack of height. If a short individual lacked confidence “Runt Personality”, the genetic offspring, when presented with proper nutrition, will potentially produce genetically tall, but psychologically small, personalities, supported by parents of equally small personality stature. If biologically small, but supported with psychologically tall personality probabilities, the individual will maintain the psychological and personality epigenetic expression.

At cultural and civilizational scale:

Environments that support certain myths will reshape the environment to propagate the same myths, while pruning other unsupported myths.

The present myths outline probabilistic pools of archetypes, and probabilistic event-based interactions.

The interactions outline probability pools of stories that can occur with the present archetypes, environments, and events that occur between these interactions.

The probability pools of stories occurring, shapes how personality develops into archetypal roles via probabilistic exposure.

The individuals likely to reproduce are shaped by these interactions, shaping the next sequence and evolution of a culture and civilization.

-
Practical Implications:

An individual can assess their current environment, patterns of possible event and story sequences, identify the roles they have played within stories (archetype), the personality within the archetype, and predict possible trajectories based upon behavioral patterns, environmental opportunities, and interactions with others.

A clearly defined civilizational or cultural myth outlines possible trajectory to follow. Synchronize archetypes, environments, and event sequences over time, to produce stories.

Most stories repeat, with clearly outlined event sequences and required archetypes (within a defined environment possibility space) to fulfill the story.

By changing any one detail within the equation, an individual can leverage their probability pool of outcomes.

Simply and tangibly; a junk food diet and lethargic pattern of behavior will not result in an active archetype or mythology, the athlete story will not be lived. Unless, an individual modifies environment, personality traits, behavioral patterns (archetype), -and- interacts with other supporting archetypes, within story-supporting sequences. In this way, an individual would have lived the lethargic to athlete story; if this story becomes known in the minds of others, and serves as replicating motivation to inspire change in others, this becomes the lethargic to athlete myth, shaping the environment to further support this myth, and cultivate further epigenetic expression.

The myth would then propagate itself.

The individual can save massive amounts of time, energy, and effort, by leveraging myth directly.

By using the inspiration from a previously lived and outlined myth, an individual can replicate, and save “guesswork”.

Replicate, (to the best of one’s ability), environment, behavior, personality traits, interactions with others, and story sequences; the myth becomes probabilistically likely to express itself, increasing with time and effort.

By modifying even the smallest of details, the probability pools of potential expression changes.
Less junk food is less likely to result in the sloth/glutton myth.
Some junk food supports the “everyman” myth.
“Everyman” myths have greater probability of achieving “the great man” myth, with adjustments.

By having knowledge of myths, components, sequences, and story progressions, individuals can assess where they have been, possible forward trajectories, and adjust towards deliberate trajectories.

Every individual that becomes a myth, decreases the investment others must take towards fulfilling their own stories, (and potentially becoming their own myths), by being a visible and replicable role model.

Understanding mythology, helps understand stories and mythology of “average people” and negative stories the average person should reasonably wish to avoid. The mythology and story of the criminal, for example, though alluring to the average, undefined, unrefined masses, is -only- alluring, due to the lack of alternative myths, and investment of environmental, biological, behavioural, and sequential social changes.
-
Write your own Story. Become Mythical. Join others in Mythology.

-Paul Hedron (Polyhedron)
Many-Faced Man

Bwick Anthony.author: Scott
Bwick Anthony.text: I do coaching and sales. Holistic self-improvement, I teach men how to do deep journaling which will allow them to teach themselves to think in a structured and logical manner, solution oriented, goal setting, reverse engineer those goals into actionable steps they can translate into a time table so they can progress towards those goals consistently, I teach them (if they are ready) about the structure of reality and how to use the most powerful tools to get them anything they want. I make men powerful by showing them how to develop any skill by undertanding the 3 components of the META skill: deliberate practice, reflection upon their practice and theoretical knowledge applied to their practice. I keep them accountable to stay consistent at their goals, teach them how to build discipline and how to improve the 3 main areas of their lives, body, mind and soul. Body by working out and learning about nutrition (trust me people have no idea about what's actually healthy in reality, did you know that vegetables are filled with anti-nutrients like phytic acid and oxalates? Oxalates bind in your blood to iron, magnesium, zinc and all metalic nutrients and render them useless for your body to consume, but I'm digressing here), mind by learning to consistently read because reading is the most powerful way to acquire knowledge in depth and of course through deep journaling they'll also develop their minds and their capacity to focus and their depth of thought will increase by 1000x, and soul by practicing meditation daily, breathwork, gratitude journaling and learning about the structure of reality, the way consciousness works and how to expand it and connect with source consciousness amongst other things. That's the quick overview of it I guess.

Rahul S.author: Scott
Rahul S.text: I haven't counted the whole thing yet since the beginning but this last month I acquired 2 clients of 1K each and I got my other 2 since the beginning (after the first month free back in December last year) and I got another client but her business is so big that I decided it would be better get paid in comission (each sales she makes reports me a 20%, but we haven't started with her project yet, she has a high ticket course I'm helping her to develop of $10K, which means each sale would be 2K for me) and I got now my course which would be done by the end of this week and ready to launch on Monday teaching educational content creators how to start making money with an audience of 0. I teach them how to build the course, we solve any issues with payment processing, I walk them through sales' training so they can do outbound sales and I even offer them the option to get comission based porfessional sales' people to do all the job for them! So it's a perfect offer!

Rahul S.author: Scott
Rahul S.text: The two new clients I acquired for my coaching this month are fitness coaches, but when I saw myself coaching for 5 hours straight every day (even though I do love the process and being there in the trenches with my boys) I realized that it was too time consuming and I had to stop and switch to a more effective way to get my guys results, so I realized I should build a course, host a community for them and build the entire business framework so we could start making money on automatic mode. I fucking love building business models and running them! Is just like running an experiment! And is all the more fun when the risks are so low and the rewards so high!

Rohan Serwer.author: Participant 1
Rohan Serwer.text:
saw your post in synthesizers. I can relate to the negative habit stacking so deeply. I used to play video games, listen to music and also scroll YT/FB in between (when I died and was waiting to be respawned). I didn't really enjoy doing them individually but these 3 habits stacked together gave me the biggest high.

Eventhough they aren't smoking or drinking redbull but I was ADDICTED to say the least. Video games induce the same dopamine as any other hard drugs so I was hooked pretty much.

Every day I craved the feeling of intense pleasure with these 3 and craved that escapism. It got to the point where it wasn't even enjoyable but I still did it repeatedly. It felt like I couldn't escape it and each night I went to bed with deep resentment and regret.

Now after a lot of trial and errors, I'm so grateful I'm over all of these negative habit stack. And I relate with you so much that I wanted to give you free help to eliminate them as well. Tell me how's the situation going with the old habits coming back

Adopt the habit of asking the cashier how their day is going, complimenting the cute barista and generally just having small talk with everyone you see in your day. A helpful tip is to adopt the 'happy retard' mentality, imagine how would a happy retard approach... by just saying the simplest thing, like hello, and having a super simple conversation. Switching your mentality to this makes it easier to approach because it blocks you from overthinking.This is super useful when cold approaching cute girls😁I can give you some more tips if you want, just send me a dm here in skool.Hope this helps bro💪

Love this, man. I also have the exact same tendancy to go on long proses and tangents when all could be said in shorter, more impactful ways.

We have a depth and thoughtfulness superpower.

Areeb Khan.author: Scott
Areeb Khan.text:
This is what I do for young men:

# Stage 1:

First we lay the foundation, I teach them how to set goals through deep journaling. You can't get where you want to go if you don't know for starters where you want to go. Through deep journaling you also teach yourself to think in an orderly, structured and logical manner and can develop actionable steps to achieve your goals, basically reverse engineer such goals into daily habits and tasks. That's where the timetable comes in. I teach them how to build their timetables to get consistent at the habits that will take them from where they are to where they want to go.

# Stage 2:

Usually we all want a strong aesthetic body and be healthy, have energy and vitality, so I teach them how to get consistent at the gym and the secrets of nutrition (must people are clueless about nutrition, for example most people think vegetables are healthy but vegetables are filled with anti-nutrients like oxalates and phytic acid, oxalates bind to iron, zing and magnesium in your blood and render them useless for you body to assimilate, that's why they are called anti-nutrients).

We all want wisdom and knowledge to make sound decisions and to know which path to take, so I teach them to get consistent and reading which is the most profound way to acquire knowledge there is.

We all want to have good mental health, control over out attention and a personal understanding of the structure of reality, that's why I teach them how to meditate and eleveate their vibrational frequency. How to do gratitude journaling consistently and to expand their consciousness.

# Stage 3:

And last but no least we need a purpose, a way, a path, a mission. I teach them how to find it and follow it. Build their online businesses, help other people (potentially as educational content creators) and once they are ready and if I see they are worthy I take them into my inner circle and get them ready for the next stage.

# Stage 4:

I'm putting together a team of high quality, powerful men into self-improvement making online more than $10K per month to kickstart a civilizational project, at the beginning a nomadic tribe of men into self-improvement off the grid, soverign. But to get into that place they need first to overcome The Trial. The initiation ritual. A 3 months journey by sea from England to Karelia, northen Russia, where we'll hunt wolves and each men will have to undergo a series of tests to prove he can become part of our brotherhood.

Kasper Bäckman.text: Hey, Kasper, yes, sorry for the late response, crazy last couple of days, a lot of work and keeping up with my self-improvement routine, you know...

Kasper Bäckman.author: Participant 2
Kasper Bäckman.text: How are you? Did you gain some value from my response? Have you tried to implement something from there?

Kasper Bäckman.author: Participant 1
Kasper Bäckman.text: Yes, staying organized has definitely helped. But the hardest thing for me right now is this big goal I have: AI. I really, truly want to make AI my lifelong career. The problem is, I keep working in short bursts, then stopping, and starting over again.

Kasper Bäckman.author: Participant 2
Kasper Bäckman.text: On the self-improvement space people always talk about the importance of discipline over motivation but I do not believe that to be as they say. What I have discovered is that you must recharge yourself with motivation periodically and strongly.

Kasper Bäckman.author: Participant 2
Kasper Bäckman.text: You must have a vision... powerful enough for you to endure tha pain of hard work consistently and relentlessly.

Kasper Bäckman.author: Participant 2
Kasper Bäckman.text: Like Nietzsche said "He who has a WHY can endure almost any HOW". I believe that to be true.

Kasper Bäckman.author: Participant 2
Kasper Bäckman.text: But to stay consistent at anything firts of all you need to lower the bar to daily sucess enough so you can hit your goal daily and get positive reinforcement early on. That's the fastest way to get consistent at anything long-term.

Kasper Bäckman.author: Participant 2
Kasper Bäckman.text: Imagine for example you want to become consistent at reading.

Kasper Bäckman.author: Participant 2
Kasper Bäckman.text: If you start by reading 50 or 100 pages of a book in one day and you force youself to do it you'll get discouraged by how hard it turns out to be and you won't be able to sustain it for very long. You'll end up resenting the activity and you'll give up on it eventually. But if instead you decide that daily sucess for you looks like reading two pages of a book daily you know you can hit that any day. And after a couple of days or a week, all of the sudden... something magical will happen: you'll read 5.

Hassan Raja.author: Scott
Hassan Raja.text: I'm putting together the offer. Someone here on skool ,aparently from my recent activity was curious about what I do and this is the way a phrased it to him, and realized I can pulish this and turn it into my offer:

Hassan Raja.author: Scott
Hassan Raja.text:
# Stage 1:
First we lay the foundation, I teach them how to set goals through deep journaling. You can't get where you want to go if you don't know for starters where you want to go. Through deep journaling you also teach yourself to think in an orderly, structured and logical manner and can develop actionable steps to achieve your goals, basically reverse engineer such goals into daily habits and tasks. That's where the timetable comes in. I teach them how to build their timetables to get consistent at the habits that will take them from where they are to where they want to go.
# Stage 2:
Usually we all want a strong aesthetic body and be healthy, have energy and vitality, so I teach them how to get consistent at the gym and the secrets of nutrition (must people are clueless about nutrition, for example most people think vegetables are healthy but vegetables are filled with anti-nutrients like oxalates and phytic acid, oxalates bind to iron, zing and magnesium in your blood and render them useless for you body to assimilate, that's why they are called anti-nutrients).
We all want wisdom and knowledge to make sound decisions and to know which path to take, so I teach them to get consistent and reading which is the most profound way to acquire knowledge there is.
We all want to have good mental health, control over out attention and a personal understanding of the structure of reality, that's why I teach them how to meditate and eleveate their vibrational frequency. How to do gratitude journaling consistently and to expand their consciousness.
# Stage 3:
And last but no least we need a purpose, a way, a path, a mission. I teach them how to find it and follow it. Build their online businesses, help other people (potentially as educational content creators) and once they are ready and if I see they are worthy I take them into my inner circle and get them ready for the next stage.
# Stage 4:
I'm putting together a team of high quality, powerful men into self-improvement making online more than $10K per month to kickstart a civilizational project, at the beginning a nomadic tribe of men into self-improvement off the grid, soverign. But to get into that place they need first to overcome The Trial. The initiation ritual. A 3 months journey by sea from England to Karelia, northen Russia, where we'll hunt wolves and each men will have to undergo a series of tests to prove he can become part of our brotherhood.

Nick Banik.text: Hey everyone! I'm Jamie from Virginia Beach in the U.S.I got into self-improvement when I signed up for fitness coaching back in 2023The pain that got me into it was that I didn't bounce back from the weight I gained while my wife was pregnant with our second kid and we both needed to lose some fat so that we could keep up with our kidsI started coaching the beginning of last year because I saw a need for people to know what I learned through it (and the cool connections that I found from my 10+ years of Bible study and how self-improvement is at the core of it)1 yearI've gone through a lot of iterations of niches, my current path (which I feel good about) is the fitness one. I tried mindset, habit formation, and identity focus all of which are suppose to be the HOW of what I deliver, not the WHAT I deliver (which took me a year to figure out 🤷‍♂️)

Nick Banik.author: Nick Banik
Nick Banik.text: Hey everyone, my name is Nick Banik, and I'm a certified men's life coach and Neuro-Linguistic Programming (NLP) Master. I am an exited founder who built a digital agency and got it acquired and then I became a day trading coach. I studied NLP throughout that and got my certifications and started working with ambitious men in life coaching who were on a similar journey that I went through. I basically wanted to create a way to work with men that I wish I had when I went through all the experiences that I did.I've been coaching for a while across disciplines, but more of the life coaching and life purpose coaching with NLP is more recent over the past several months after getting certified. I'm currently acquiring clients in this capacity for 1 on 1, and it's going well. I seem to be attaching entrepreneurs, which makes sense because that's my background.I'm happy to be here, fam!

I love learning from the way of the samurai. They were so dynamic in developing mind, body, and spirit through relentless physical training, intellectual studying, creative and artistic development through art, music, and writing, and they connected deeply to God through meditation, prayer, and other practices. I strive to be this way in my own life through playing and writing music, doing dynamic activities like martial arts, snowboarding, trail running, etc, studying and learning things, and practicing my craft all while staying consistent everyday. We can learn so much from the samurai.

Nick Banik.author: Lead

I've already worked with high ticket business coaches before, so it's not really that appealing to me at the moment because I've spent a lot with them. It's usually the same stuff with sales script, offer refinement, defining the niche and messaging, going all in on a couple of market channels, etc and I'm trying to take a more unique approach to this business because I did all that with my last business. For this one, I want everything to feel aligned.

Nick Banik.author: Scott
Nick Banik.text:
I was thinking more about having a conversation, not a sales’ call, since you told me you were not interested on my offer I discarded that already, but regardless I’m always interested on getting to know the guys on my community and fostering a space for REAL and authentic connections here.

To make it clear, first proposal for a call was a sales’ call.

This one is just to chat, chill and get to know each other, bro.

My intention is to be always straightforward. I know that might come off as too strong or intense sometimes but I wholeheartedly believe is the best approach to live as a whole in the long term. Wouldn’t you agree?

Nick Banik.author: Lead
Nick Banik.text: Oh gotcha, I am sorry I thought you were just trying to have another sales call. I am so used to all the outreach from business coaches that my guard was up. And yeah I do this in my own community, I just have free coffee hour, no agenda calls sometimes. I would love to do that and just have a chill call to get to know each other.

Nick Banik.author: Scott
Nick Banik.text:
I mean, imagine that… we ordered food, meditated together and when we finished up the meditation the food was at the door.
I remember thinking the whole time “holy shit this is the dream!”. I mean… a couple of years ago… even dreaming about inviting my friends to meditate with me or anything remotely close like a breathwork session, a sound healing experience would have been impossible! And I tried!!!
The best I would be able to get them to do with me was working out and sprints but they’d fall off pretty quickly.
But now… now I’m surrounded by the kind of people who invite ME to do the things I fucking love!
This path is really a massive gift and so often we forget to recognize it or we start taking it for granted and normalize it but this is pure magic and such an extraordinary experience I don’t want to stop being amazed by it.

Nick Banik.author: Lead

Hanna Urban.author: Rob Nölken
Hanna Urban.text: Hi fellow human!My name is Rob from the Netherlands, almost 53 winters young.In 2018 I had for the 3th time an inflammation and Perferation of my intestines. They had to take 35 cm out. My beloved lifestyle of sex, drugs and Rock n Roll almost took a toll on me.A year later I struggled with a burnout. I had to Get Sh!t Done! Wich is the name of my self developed course and community.I developed the course first of all for me, to keep me accountable and to work on the best version of myself. But I started to help other people aswell. In 2020 I started Disorganized Productions and Workinprogress as a company.I have everything set up, website, affiliate marketing, and do Podcasts (Chaos creates Harmony). I have a certificate as a "expert of experience" and do a study now to become a Holistic Health expert.Helping others to become the best version of themselves is my life purpose.I live, since almost a year in my Volvo(Ragnar) as a car-nomad, wich is the best time I ever!Mirroring, facing, embracing and loving my demons was the most important thing I did. And the most painful aswel. Facing and working on my trauma's, never ending story, haha.I think that Skool is the best platform to help me, so I can help others, to become the best version of themselves.Change life, before it changes you!Here is a little video about me:https://youtu.be/564W2ncmN8A?=siA8VPq2pFJlb7PrdcLove to connect with you, fellow human!Warm geertings,Rob3:42

Hanna Urban.author: Scott Northwolf
Hanna Urban.text: @Hanna Urban I got into self-improvement after a 4 year long chronic depression... after only two weeks following my timetable, working out, reading and meditating consistently and cutting out all instant gratification activities that would spike my dopamine levels I got out of the depression... I truly wish more people knew about this... I guess that's why we do what we do. I couldn't think of a more meaningful and profoundly powerful work than quite LITERALLY saving lives and helping people change radically their lives for the better.

Hanna Urban.author: Hanna Urban
Hanna Urban.text: @Scott Northwolf exactly. Every day my mind is blown away by the power we possess within. The more we experience (and yes, the hardcore experience have the ability to accelerate the speed of our realization), the easier it becomes to believe it and witness its power.We are incredible beings 😍

Hanna Urban.author: Hanna Urban
Hanna Urban.text: Hi everyone,For me it goes like this:.It started with a doctor directing me to meditation and yoga instead of prescribing medications. (Yes, there are luckily doctors like that 🙏)Years ago I was suffering from depression and that was the beginning of it all for meThe more I dove into this journey of physical connection (yoga had more and faster effect on me), the more I felt at home. I started discovering spirituality form totally different angle and started to broaden my view.I started in 2021 but it wasn't a straight journey (i guess it never is...😅)Fully on board, restarting my business as I took needed time after my father's death

Bharti Singh.author: Scott
Bharti Singh.text:
Hi,

I’m currently building something deeply personal: an emotionally intelligent AI companion designed to let people talk without fear of judgment — like having a friend, therapist, or confidant in your pocket who truly listens.

With mental health challenges and loneliness on the rise, I believe technology can offer not just connection, but emotional safety and healing.

I’m at the early MVP stage and would be truly grateful for any advice, feedback, or even a quick chat about how to shape this into something impactful. Your experience in [AI / mental health / startups] would mean a lot to me as I take the next steps.

Would you be open to a 15-minute call or even just sharing a few thoughts over here?

Thank you so much,
Bharti Singh

Sajjad Bablu.author: Sajjad Bablu
Sajjad Bablu.text: Over the last weeks I’ve been studying something interesting:  Most people online are creating content for visibility Very few are creating systems for transformation  Likes don’t change a person. Motivation videos don’t change behavior. Even good advice rarely sticks.  So I started experimenting with a different approach.  Instead of posting random self-improvement content, I’m building a structured flow:  1. A problem people recognize immediately 2. A guided reflection 3. A daily actionable step 4. Consistency over motivation  The goal isn’t engagement. The goal is clarity + behavior change.  Right now I’m testing how people respond when the focus shifts from inspiration → guidance  Not selling anything here — just observing behavior patterns and learning what actually helps people follow through in real life.  Curious: Do you think people actually want transformat

Sajjad Bablu.author: Scott Northwolf
Sajjad Bablu.text: This is very interesting, brother. Hope it goes well and please do share what you find out with this approach.Personally I have discovered that people do not value what they get for free for the most part.True transformation I have only seen it taking place through high ticket programs because then they are putting skin on the game, they are committed to make a change and there's real world consecuences to them not doing so (especially if you structure your offer with a powerful full money back conditional guarantee or even a risk reversal one which are usually the best but only when you get to a certain level on the game).This is a clear breakdown of it with some estimate projections that reflect this point:📊 Success (Completion) Rates by Program TypeProgram TypeTypical Completion Rate (Success Proxy)Free programs~5–15 % complete Low-ticket programs (e.g., paid but no heavy support)~15–40 % complete High-ticket programs (e.g., with coaching/community support)~60–80 %+ completeI actually took the time to dig into this data because I have been seeing this patter for a while and I was wondering if there were any studies about it and there are!It's insane but it's true!Real transformation happens when people are in enough pain so their current situation and staying in it is actually more painful than the effort and sacrifice of taking action consistently and changing radically their lives.I hope understanding this helps you to focus your efforts on the right direction, brother, because I did struggle with this for quite a while, running a lot of free case studies that would end up with VERY high churn and barely any results out of a lack of work and consistency from my mentees but reality is how it is, and I could have sat down and complained about it but instead I decided to accept reality as it is and actually look for the most effective approach to make a meaningful and profound impact on people's lives.If you want to make such impact you either need insane amounts of traffic so this low completion rates get to represent a lot more people than on low traffic enviroments, or you have to switch to a model in which the low traffic is actually very successful because the system is built in such a way that is conductive to VERY high standards and results driven by the level of committment of people (which is what I do and what I personally focus on).Delusion backed up by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brother. Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!

Sajjad Bablu.author: Sajjad Bablu

Once the right structure is clear, monetization and commitment layers can be added on top of it.I’m testing the foundation before scaling the pressure.Will definitely share the results here.

Braden S Watt.text:
Hey Scott, welcome to Skool.Group!

Do you already have a course/coaching program? Or are you looking to launch a community here on Skool?

Braden S Watt.author: Scott

Lee Simmons.author: Lead
Lee Simmons.text: Hi Scott! It read like you have a question for me?

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: Many...

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: are you up for a quick call now? I'll share you a google meet link if you are..

Lee Simmons.author: Lead
Lee Simmons.text: I don't go off platform

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: I heard skool had set up an in-platform video call system but apparently they haven't set that up yet for calls on the chat for what I can tell.

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: But that's fine.

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: I hardly know how to ask you this question but I'll give it my best shot on a chat, it's way easier on a video call or in person...

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: So... who are you?

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: What are you?

Lee Simmons.author: Lead
Lee Simmons.text: Who am I? I am . I am is who I am. I just go by Lee. What am I? I'm a Star Seed who chose to come to Earth to help people and the Earth.

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: When we dream what happens is that consciousness turns into the dream itself but consciousness (as you must know) cannot experience itself without creating an illusion of separation, so we become one person inside this dream, with limitations, capable only to experience one part of the dream and not its entirety even though we are all of it. That's exactly what happens on this plain of reality. That's why we can we can lucid dream and that's why we can manifest in this other plain. I believe you know that. I believe you are aware of it... am I right?

Lee Simmons.author: Lead
Lee Simmons.text: Yes I am aware of this. I have had lucid dreams and been manifesting since childhood

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: You can access the astral plain and channel information directly from source. I know this to be possible but I have never experienced it myself. I know of those who can do astral projection and such things. I'd like to request something from you if you'd be as kind as to indulge me on this.

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: It might sound kind of trivial but I'd be eternally grateful if you could do this for me. Can you do a demostration of telepathy for me now?

Lee Simmons.author: Lead
Lee Simmons.text: Why do you want one? What are you wanting to find out?

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: Why would anyone want to experience something magical they haven't experienced yet? Obviously curiosity is a powerful motivator. I want to know. I want to see.

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: What do I want to find out? Whatever you can show me.

Lee Simmons.author: Lead
Lee Simmons.text: Being skeptical of things you don't experience yourself like telepathy, empathic and psychic ability is healthy and normal

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: I know.

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: It's just like being skeptical of anything you don't know. You're steping into the unknown and the unknown is chaos. But every time you face chaos with the heroic attitude you bring forth the creative and rejuvenating powers out of it. So that's what I intend to do.

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: That's what I have been doing all this time.

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: That's why I'm requesting this of you, if you are willing to help me...

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: so will you?

Lee Simmons.author: Lead
Lee Simmons.text: I have already started. That comment about your skepticism was from your mind

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: ok, can we take it a step further? Can you tell me what's the name of the video on Youtube I just watched right now? I can stare at it if you need me to right now...

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: I know it's a lot to ask and I perfectly undertand if you are not willing to or can't. No worries. I just had to ask...

Lee Simmons.author: Lead
Lee Simmons.text: For things that specific, I need to be in proximity to you

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: I understand. Can you tell me what's my biggest fear on this seasson of my life?

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: And be as especific as you can?

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: *specific

Lee Simmons.author: Lead
Lee Simmons.text: You fear that you will be, for lack of a better term, a casualty of the wold chaos. You fear you and yours won't survive what is coming

Lee Simmons.author: Lead
Lee Simmons.text: world

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: Am I facing my fear?

Lee Simmons.author: Lead
Lee Simmons.text: Yes. You are preparing as best you can, but fear no amount of preparing and organizing with others will be enough. Chaos has the numbers

Lee Simmons.author: Scott
Lee Simmons.text: Thank you, Lee. That was very helpful. We'll talk again.

Lee Simmons.author: Lead
Lee Simmons.text: Sure thing!

Vol. & Compression --> Normalise --> -1.0dB
Compressor: Threshold of -12.0dB
Filter Curve EQ: Treble Boost and also Bass Boost
Normalise again

I have gotten 0 value from our coaching so far. I’m not going to lie. It’s just not working at all and I haven’t seen you putting in any effort. You can keep what I paid you but I hope you improve this for future clients, man. I’m sorry but I have to tell you because I don’t think other peoples are going to be as understanding and chill about this as me and I don’t want you to run into trouble out there, man. You need to put in way more effort and show clients you really care about getting them results and make them feel like they have gotten real value and worth out of their money, because me? I feel like I threw my money down the drain to be honest. I could have used that money for editing and thumbnail edits and would have gotten something way more tangible. When I got started with coaching I did one month for free, one to three hours calls with my clients every single day, including weekends and holidays, I was that committed, and I wasn’t even charging… imagine how much more effort and intensity I placed when I started charging… and I started charging only 142€, less than what you charged me, and we have jumped on 3 calls of 30 minutes to 1 hour and I don’t even have your phone number which shows a big lack of commitment and trust with your clients. And if you don’t trust your clients… how do you expect them to trust you? I hope this helps and you don’t take it the wrong way. But I can’t lie to you and say I’m very happy with what I got. I’m profoundly dissatisfied and disappointed, bro.

Cris Morales.text: I'm all in the strategy of comments, chats, posts, dms, etc. But I do sometimes go to make my daily contributions in a group, and end up leaving a "congrats man!". Sometimes, there is nothing else to say.  Sometimes, groups aren't super active, or they aren't talking about what I know I can help them with. My question to myself and to you guys is  If there is nothing to say, should we even say it? Or should we ALWAYS be making ourselves visible, chatting, commenting, posting in the group, even though it doesn't have much to do with your niche, offer, solution, etc?

Cris Morales.author: Scott Northwolf
Cris Morales.text: Less is more.You cannot pour from an empty cup.Fill up your cup.Go read. Learn something new. Meditate. Workout. Stay in silence for a day or two and stop doing outreach (I know it sounds counterproductive but it's what has worked for me).Then you go back only when you have something to say, after a day or two you will...And then magic will happen.Remember that you don't have to do this... you GET to do this! And that's a beautiful priviledge that we have. If we are not having fun and enjoying it, and feeling exactly how we would feel on a 9 to 5 or something like that... then what's the point?Like Nietzsche said "A man can bear almost any HOW as long as he has a WHY."You must see the point in this, otherwise you'll burnout.But sometimes pushing to our limits and actually trying hard (especially to build discipline with regards to outreach and networking) will give you also the beautiful opportunity to experience that sweet contrast between the two approaches and as usual... the most effective way lies somewhere in the middle, when you strike that perfect balance and fluctuate between the two.Life is rhythmic and trying to force reality into one stale structure will always result in dissapointment.Detatch yourself from expectations and learn to enjoy the journey.I know it sounds like a cliche but it has been said by so many wise men for a reason.It is true.I hope this helps, brother.

Lukas Schmidt.author: Lukas Schmidt
Lukas Schmidt.text:
Since I joined Skool back in 2024, I wanted to win the Skool Games.
It is the closest thing to the business Olympics there is.
But that also means you compete against the best.
The best have a massive audience with teams and systems in place to scale a low-ticket offer.
(In the Skool Games, you can charge a maximum of $100 per month or $1200 per year)
I worked a lot more in 26 Q1 compared to 25 Q4, but made a lot less.
Still grateful for the experience and had a lot of fun.
But I wouldn't recommend it to anyone who is getting started.
In the beginning, it is much more effective to charge high ticket and scale as much as possible.
Instead of chasing the shiny trophy, I am committed to building a solid foundation.
I also found it to be much easier to help someone else win.
Congrats again to @Michael Rizk and @Jenna Kelly.
You guys crushed it.
I am also excited to be more active here again, since I am not locked into the competition.
Let's change the world together.
What are you focused on in Q2?

Lukas Schmidt.author: Scott Northwolf
Lukas Schmidt.text: I couldn't agree more, low-ticket is going to be harder to pull off, especially in the beginning because it requires massive amounts of traffic. With leverage and money you can build up that traffic and if you get that from high ticket that's the best way to go.

Andreas Leijonmarck.author: Scott Northwolf
Andreas Leijonmarck.text: If your offer is a one liner like "I take self-improvement coaches from $0 to $10K per month in 42 days with my 'Reverse Engineered $10K Method' or they don't pay" then you do tell it even to your neighboor because you lose nothing and create massive curiousity by putting it out there every time you open your mouth.Creating content is the slowest and most inefficient way to get calls but it's the best for the long-term, be prepared to do it consistently and daily if possible for 7 months to 3 years.Cold and warm outreach is the fastest and most powerful way because you get to target precisely your niche, but that, of course, assumes you have locked in PERFECTLY your niche, you know exactly how they look, where they hang out, what's their language and their desired outcome. You need to be able to describe their day to day from the top of your head if I were to ask you at a moment's notice.Running ads with a proven startegy and making less thatn $50K per month is basically insanity, I have seen it a thousand times and some of the best players in the market keep saying it, if you don't have $10K every month that you can literally BURN and forget about them testing different startegies for ads you are throwing money into the trash bin.The ICP is still too broad and doesn't give emotional triggers to the potential audience but it's a good place to start, it's definetely better than nothing.This is the no bullshit answer and I know it sounds harsh but I hope you can take it objectively and analyze these points.I hope it helps, brother.Delusion backed up just by words it's just delusion but delusion backed up by relentless action... that's called FAITH, brotner! Remember: Duty, Honor and Pride!!!

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: I'd like to know more about your Impact Framework... 🙂

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: Do you've got like a nice VSL for me or something?

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: By the way, check out mine on my Skool community, bro, I'd appreciate the feedback, since you know so much about marketing and all...

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Lead
Pepijn Verheijen.text: Sure! It's basically a framework that gets people who are stuck below 10k/m to 20k - 30k a month. I don't have a VSL yet.

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: VSLs boost conversion rates by a lot. Is not that hard to record, I thought it would be harder, I took inspiration from one I saw from a program I bought recently. Really good stuff, is a sales program called Alpha Setters...

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: How does your framework works? I mean, if you are not afraid of spilling too much sauce on me... of course.

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: I'm more of the Alex Hormozi approach, I give away all the secrets and charge for the implementation. It's working pretty well for me so far...

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Lead
Pepijn Verheijen.text: Ah, I see man. Okay so my framework is basically everything that you need. What I'll do is I look at your business, creat finacial models for your current stats, make analyses to see where you lack potential and I'll improve. I basically go over your ENTIRE funnel to see what you can improve and I will use my impact strategies to solve problems and bottlenecks. How does that sound?

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: Pretty good. How good an offer is sometimes depends entirely on how strong the guarantee is. Do you have any? The best ones are reverse guarantee (risk inverse guarantee, but I have only seen the really really top dogs do that one) and the next best one is comission based guarantee. But there are some others that are also pretty good.

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Lead
Pepijn Verheijen.text: I have a guarantee. I will make sure that you at least 2x your current income within 120 days, if not, 100% money back.

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Lead
Pepijn Verheijen.text: Yo wassup man!

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: What's up, bro?

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Lead
Pepijn Verheijen.text: Yooo my man! How is life going for you?

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: Very busy. I don't have enough hours in the day to do all the work I want to do. Instead of playing this game of life in first person I think I need to play it like a startegy game, micro-managing a lot of characters or something to actually hit my goals... What about you?

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Lead
Pepijn Verheijen.text: Yeah got that man, it’s about putting in the consistent actions in order to get the Needle moving yk. Been great for me aswell man, but yeah time management is hard atm

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: Also, the most important thing is to find people you can actually trust and delegate to. I'm having a really hard time finding reliable people to do business with... for example, right now one of my business partners and I are hiring teachers for our online tutoring company and we haven't come across a single one that is actually serious about it, shows up in time and we can actually rely on... despite the fact that we are paying really well!

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Lead
Pepijn Verheijen.text: Aha, yeah man, totaly agree with that, there are so many people who communicate in a way with only the goal of selling yk. Ofc you don't trust me now, that's normal. You need to make sure that you are being trusted. Because the person you are reflects on the people you attract yk. Make sure you become the trust guy aswell!

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Lead
Pepijn Verheijen.text: Sick btw that you have this tutoring company, what is it about?

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: tutoring, online tutoring for high school guys on programming, math, chemistry and physics.

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: I wasn't really talking about trust on those terms, not really about the trust you need to build with a potential customer, I meant trust in the people you are hiring, trust in the fact that they will actually do the work you are paying them to do.

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Lead
Pepijn Verheijen.text: Yeah I know man, that is hard, you can make a horse go to the water, but you can't force him to drink it. You can do things like daily checkins or criteria that they need to do in a day. But yeah man, trust is everything nowadays, and it is the hardest to acquire

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: By any chance do you know any high school teachers that speak good English and want to work online? We are paying 14,4$ per hour for starters, but we will pay even more as time goes by and the teacher proves to be serious and of high quality. If they work part time...

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: so 3 hours per day, 5 days per week week they can make with us 864$ in a month...

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: but if they do it full time, 9 to 5 they can get to 2.5K per month... and that's just at the beginning, like I said...

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Scott
Pepijn Verheijen.text: We are looking for teachers, but even advanced students will do if they are up for it and interested and of course if they are professional and serious. Do you know anybody who could be interested?

Pepijn Verheijen.author: Lead

I keep doing my coaching 1 on 1 for self-improvement and online business consulting which is the only thing that has actually paid off so far, but I only got one paying client, the rest are dead-broke but since I see potential in them I have been working with them for free but I'm honestly super tired of working so much for free... that might be the reason I'm finally feeling like I'm burning out... Maybe I should just focus on coaching, getting paid per session instead of monthly, do outbound sales and get myself 10 clients and stop doing a daily hour like I have been doing which is an insane amount of work and I'm tired as fuck, and instead just do 1 hour per week with each client. I have been thinking about this model for a while and if I get myself 10 clients paying me 60$ per session weekly I could hit 2.4K per month and finally have some breathing space because I feel like I'm drowning lately, working until 6 AM every single day and night and I'm still dead broke, living by myself on the quite the opposite side of the world, on a strange as fuck culture. William Tu.author: Scott
William Tu.text: I can help you with the business side of things, mainly on how to build the funnel, how to structure the offer. This is the best framework usually: You start runnign your MVP as a coaching service for a sort of low ticket, you gain momentum and confidence, build up some testimonials and acquire enough knowlege of your niche and your dream avatar so you can actually develop a course. Usually the sweet spot is after 15 clients with results but it can be more or less depending on some factors. Then you'll realize that your niche (if you're focusing on one niche and one niche only instead of trying to help everyone that comes your way, I'll explain in more detail why it doesn't work and why you can't hit 10K per month doing that) has certain recurrent problems, your brain will start doing some patterm recognition, I can teach you the structure to build the course, you set it up on skool (it's the most effective and easier option) but the kowledge of the content will develop organically in your brain. Then you set up the coures and with enough traffic from you YT channel you sell it and now the coaching becomes a high ticket thing becasue you have developed such authority on your niche. Then you run adds for both the course and your high ticket, one at a time and after you have gained some knowledge on how to do that or either you buy a tool to do it or hire a marketing agency, there's many options here. At this point you scale from 10 or 30K per month to 100K which is usually the ceiling for low-ticket courses combined with high ticket coaching, top 150K or so. After that there's other levels, but let's concern ourselves with the current one. How much are you making currently (how many clients and how much are you charging per client?) and what's the traffic that you get usually from one video?

Joseph Cook.author: Lead

Hey Scott! Haha thanks I did not know that about my name 😆

That’s awesome man, how did you initially get into that field?

What’s been your biggest takeaways from working with these type of individuals?

Is this something you still do? How has your service changed over the years. 📈

Joseph Cook.author: Scott
Joseph Cook.text: I got first into self-improvement to escape pain. I went through a 4 year long chronic depression that led me to suicidal thoughts and the way out of it I found it through self-improvement, building a timetable for myself and focusing on developing body (through working out), mind (by reading) and spirit (through meditation and gratitude journaling) and after only 2 weeks I was out of the depression and I decided to never stop improving consistently my life, 1% better every single day.

Joseph Cook.author: Scott
Joseph Cook.text: Takeaways from my clients and my work? Well there’s a lot to tell about that. I don’t want to extend myself endlessly for the sake of time and efficiency so I’ll try to say only one takeaway and summarize it as much as I can here: Basically I realized that the biggest constraint to deliver to my clients their desired outcome was… *drumrolls… themselves! I knew I could take them from where they were to where they wanted to be in (let’s say) 3 months, but still would take me 7 to actually get to a meaningful outcome with them. The main problem was that instead of them turning off their brains and following my instructions to the letter and doing the work they would procrastinate, fight back with me and force me to convince them instead of being efficient and just doing what I would teach them. The solution I found was instead of having monthly retainers with them was to create this pain behind them, this powerful motivator for them to act, which was a conditional guaranteed on a high ticket offer. Example: I’ll take you from point A to point B in X (timeframe) or I’ll give you a full refund (under the condition that you follow my instructions and do the work consistently [I got systems in place now for a more efficient accountability process]. And that would increase the success rate and reduce the amount of time it’d take me to deliver them results.

Joseph Cook.author: Scott
Joseph Cook.text:
And no, I don’t have anymore a holistic self-improvement coaching business aimed at high level entrepreneurs and elite athletes as I used to.

I have transitioned now into business consulting for self-improvement coaches.

Basically my current offer, as you might have seen on my skool profile is:

I help self improvement coaches to go from 0 to $10K per month in 42 days with my ‘Reverse Engineered $10K Method’ or they don’t pay.

Andrew Brooks.text: How often do you go out, eat somewhere, and think to yourself "well, that wasn't great." And how did you feel afterwards?  There are reasons for the generally low quality of food in most restaurants. Mostly they are limited by their food budget. And the food budget limits the quality they are able to purchase because we also generally want cheap food. What that means is that most of the produce will be conventional produce, not organic. The meats (a large part of the budget) will be of lower quality and the rest will be medium quality at best.  The things we WANT: organic, fresh, healthy, and delicious, simply are not available within the cheap food framework. Unfortunately to get the good stuff you have to pay more. That's just the way it is for now...31Last comment Feb 24

It doesn't have to be a big thing.But I do plan fun things intentionally. I keep track of the artsy/cultural things happening in my area and will often just show up to some of those and see if I can chat with a new person there. And I will sometimes invite people I meet to connect with me at the event. Sometimes they show, sometimes not. But I'm ok doing things by myself too.So don't feel weird. You aren't. Especially as we get older, the number of friends typically dwindles, but the QUALITY of the friendships goes up. And that's ok.

I have some foundational principles that I teach from, but I do it in a way that people/clients can easily implement. I take a long term view of promoting health through good nutrition and it has to be delicious and enjoyable too.

Andrew Brooks.author: Lead
Andrew Brooks.text: and makes it sustainable. I don't coach in the way everyone else does, by removing all the things you can't have anymore. I actually use that as a base to work from. Certainly I tweak and modify, but I focus more on adding good nutrition to balance out, instead of taking everything away. Again, enjoyment must be present for at least some of the time or they won't do it at all.

Britt Smith.author: Lead

Britt Smith.author: Scott
Britt Smith.text: Advance reselling? That's interesting. Could you elaborate on that?

Britt Smith.author: Lead
Britt Smith.text: Reselling is like running an online store without actually stocking or shipping the products. Instead, you partner with suppliers who handle the inventory and shipping. When a customer buys something from your store, the supplier ships it directly to the customer. You make a profit by selling the product for more than what you pay the supplier.

Britt Smith.author: Scott
Britt Smith.text: So... droppshiping, right?

Britt Smith.author: Lead
Britt Smith.text: Yeah

Britt Smith.author: Lead

Britt Smith.author: Scott
Britt Smith.text: No thanks, bro, I don't "do" side hustles, I only have ONE MAIN mission and I dedicate 100% of my energy to it, that's why I'm so good at it and that's why I'll become the best. Plan Bs are for losers who defeat themselves before they even begin in their own minds, I either make it my way or die trying, that's why I'm having sucess already. But thanks for the advice and good luck on your sales journey, but if you want a piece of solid advice on sales, never DM people directly, build trust first in the comments, make posts, interact for real in the communities, actually build geniune connections with people, don't try to sell right off the bat, think on terms of the other person's interests, not on yours and your sale. Trust me that's how you'll make real money with sales. If you want some additional training on it I'd recommend you to read How To Win Friends and Influence People and if you want to ask me somthing about it I'd be glad to help you out with any piece of advice I can come up with. Good luck.

Cold calling is so fucking hard that I love it because it forces me to build discipline, Youtube allows me a creative outlet and hopefully in the future will turn into a massive source of traffic for my business...

So basically I got a low ticket course here on skool I'm selling for educational content creators who don't have an audience yet but want to start making money as soon as possible. I teach them on my course outbound sales so they can get clients on their offers. If they don't have a course yet I teach them how to run their MVP (minimum viable product) as a coaching service and with the feedback and results they get for their clients they can build organically a course that actually works and delivers results to their clients. All of this while they are still building their personal brands for long term traffic (because building a personal brand on IG and YT takes time). I'm currently selling my course for $250. The possition I'm offering is as appointment setter commission based, 40%, so per close you'd be getting $100. I'm extremely good at closing so If you bring me 2 to 3 people for a closing call per day I could easily close at least 1 per day. That would be (if you only worked 5 days per week, but me personally I have insane work ethics so I'd work 7 days per week if you can get me closing calls every day) 500 dollars for you per week, which in a month could be 1500 to 2000. With that you could move here to the South Asian East and live like a king! I'm currently only making around 1.7K per month and look how I'm living, in a fucking penthhouse, with two pools, a gym and meeting daily literal millionaires, digital nomads and entrepenuers from all around the world! Next week I'll be in Bali at a friend's villa, I met that guy here! This is insane, bro! I hope you make the best out of this opportunity, is really good and it's not just great training but also an insane chance to start living the dreamlife. Like I said, this place just costed me around 700 per month, food here is cheap as fuck, everything is incredibly cheap and the quality of life is even better than in the US. I mean, is insane! I only require from you that you are 100% commited, professional, show up for our calls in time, do not make excuses or ghost on me, and carry your own weight and bring results. I ask for no experience, the fact that you are on Alpha Setters is already a good sign. And of course is importnat that you have a true killer's mentality and you are willing to learn, that you have a growth mentality instead of a fixed one, if you know what I mean. We are all into self-improvement here and we are all pushing each other to grow and become the best version of ourselves. So if you are down for all of that I'll give you a time for us to make a call as soon as possible and if everything goes well we'll do the onboarding ASAP and get you into the grind right there and then. I truly wish more people knew about this... I guess that's why we do what we do. Never know what time I am done. In the evening taking a walk, do some more work (website, email and accounting) then hit the bed early . Before I go to sleep I always meditate. Even if there was a party, I always do meditate.

Rob Nölken.author: Scott Northwolf
Rob Nölken.text: @Rob Nölken I love the flow… sounds quite natural and effortless.There are two main modes of approaching your routine (neither is better, they depend on what’s your goal and what are you optimizing for):1-Intentional routine (a routine you design yourself with your goals in mind).Pros: you can reverse engineer your goal into actionable steps that you can take every day and it’s impossible to not hit it with enough time and understanding how to reiterate your process and take in feedback).Cons: It can suffocate inspiration if not properly managed or balanced with seasons of rest.2-Unintentional routine (it’s basically the routine that forms when we are adapting [effectively and productively or ineffectively and counterproductively] to our environment and internal state).Pros: Great for creativity and inspiration.Cons: It can turn into instant gratification, chaos and too much of a lack of structure that doesn’t allow for consistency or the creation and maintenance of anything (so goals are quite hard to hit in this way.And of course you can even have a combination of the two…

Demetar Kalev.author: Scott
Demetar Kalev.text: I started making self-improvement content in general, then niched down for a couple of weeks into identity shifting and meditation, then pivoted to online business, then I started sharing the channel between the 3 core self improvement habits (meditation, reading and working out) and online buisiness strategies, sharing my insights and what has been working for me, back at that time I also talked a little about nutrition.

Basically talking about the issue of Wokism and all that and inspiring the creation of a new kind of brotherhood, a nomadic tribe that could escape the decadence of the Western World we are experiencing and build a brand new civilization. That's my highest aim. I have done some vlogs now that I'm starting to make enough money online to get to travel, stay in hotels and work from there and that kind of stuff, and soon I'll be dropping some sauce because I'll be making content for us, educational content creators, basically explaining how I managed to start making money without a big following.

Demetar Kalev.author: Scott
Demetar Kalev.text: And the crazy part? I started only 4 months ago posting consistently on YT and actually trying to make money online... now things are blowing out of proportion and I was actually ready to grind at this for YEARS without seeing any results! But results are flooding in now and I can barely believe it yet... life is crazy, man. Sometimes it truly surprises you.

Demetar Kalev.author: Scott
Demetar Kalev.text: With the traffic I got now I could make 1 or 2 sales per month from my YT channel. The reason I'm making money is because I'm generating myself traffic by doing outbound sales at the same time I play for the long game of building a personal brand through my YT channel.

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: Do you know Zlatibor District?

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: I was planning to move there.

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: A couple of years back, when my online business took off, I was deciding where I was going to travel and for some reason Serbia was one of my first choices.

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: I was envisioning myself on a cabin in the mountains somewhere on Zlatibor District...

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: For some reason I ended up not going there. I ended up deciding for South America first, then Turkey and from there I went to Malaysia.

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: From Malaysia my plans were to go to Thailand but a guy I met in Kuala Lumpur told me about this island in Indonesia called Bali and sent me the number of his visa agent, so I ended up getting a Digital Nomad's visa for Indonesia and came here to Bali but I wasn't really planning to stay long but when I arrive... OMG...

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Lead
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: What a story!!! I'm even surprised you looked into Serbia and Zlatibor district is so specific, I admire people who research small places in random countries and find what they absolutely love! Your journey sounds incredible, I hope you had fun in all the countries you've been to😍 And Bali is definitely much more beautiful than Zlatibor trust me🥹🥹🥹🥹 I think the universe led you somewhere you're really supposed to be, at least for some time if not forever and I love the impression you got of Bali instantly🤩

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Lead
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: But really, in a few years, if you still want to come to Serbia and explore a bit, you're very welcome here🤞🏼

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Lead

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: so as it happens... very soon after arriving here in Bali I had a bike accident and that night I met this guy called Nikola (he's from Serbia but he's not the main character of the story, the main character is still about to reveal)... 😂

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: So he helped me out after the accident and stuff.

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: My accident happened on my way to a co-working space very famous here in Canggu called Tribal I really wanted to go.

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: By the way, even though I had the accident... I still went to Tribal... the bike was destroyed (so was I 😂) but I still could walk so I walked there... the mission always comes first.

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: I met him there at Tribal.

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: Anyways... I don't want to get into all the details of the accident because that's a truly epic story worthy of its very own time and space to be told...

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: but you can imagine I made quite an impression by entering into Tribal dripping blood all over the white floor like if I just came out of a war with the sun setting in the background. It was quite amazing.

Anastasija Kulundzic.author: Scott
Anastasija Kulundzic.text: As a matter of fact... I got kind of famous at the place. And I started going every day and started meeting a lot of really cool people. We'd have incredible conversations about self-improvement, online businesses and spirituality.

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: It was honestly a synchronicity, Kate. I wasn't really looking for setters because I have had pretty bad experiences so far... a friend of mine had to leave Bali recently and go back to Italy and he was hating it so much... this is truly paradise...

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: so I thought, since my business is going so well and I'm flooded with work I could use an appointment setter to start delegating some of that work as soon as possible...

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: So I decided to jump on a call with him...

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: but he didn't show up for the call. So I knew I couldn't trust him for that. Still love him and this does not affect our relationship at all but business is buisness and I cannot play around with it, I must be responsible about it and my standards are that if you are not serious enough to show up on time to our calls... well, I cannot trust you for such and important thing like handling my community and doing the appointment setting.

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: I mentioned this to a friend of mine while we were having lunch...

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: and he recommended me a day later one of his old clients (he is a spirituality coach) saying that she has the perfect energy, an amazing mindset, boundless gratitude, coachable and super curious to learn and smart.

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: So I told him that he could give her my number and she immediately started texting me, we organized our call and she was so enthusiastic about it, loved the work I'm doing, how I'm changing my clients' lives so radically, all the structure of the company and everything... so I hired her around a week ago... and she has been working non-stop and helping me with the marketing and promotion for this webinar we are hosting with a special guest on my community... she has been doing great and so far I'm training her for appointment setting and she's starting to use the KPIs tracker and everything... so now we just have to keep doing the work. I know she'll make it happen soon! I can't wait to see her bringing more calls to my calendar soon... it's been amazing so far working together. Her energy is magical!

He sold to him his Consulting Accelerator (I'm also going to go through that one after finishing up Charlie's, already saw the webinar in which Sam presented it 5 years ago... it's EXACTLY the same thing as Imperium Academy and you can even see how skool was born from that course)...

Kate Capelli.author: Scott

Kate Capelli.text: I got into it and learnt everything and instinctively decided to use the same methodology to help the niche I selected because getting to $10K per month was exactly what they told me repetedly niche research interview after niche research interview... and guess what? Now I realized that only 3 people have done this and used this exact method on this exact market... Sam Ovens (in 4 years made $65 millions and he's now the owner and founder of skool and a billionaire), Charlie Morgan (in 7 years he made $40 millions and he's probably going to become a billionaire as well, it's just a matter of time), and me (in only 3 months I went from $0 to more than $20K and growing)...

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: and funny enough... when I set the time for my program (42 days) I didn't even realize that Charlie's program is 6 weeks which guess what? It's 42 days! And Sam Oven's old program is...

Basically IA doesn't have Charlie doing one on one with you every single day throughout the entire thing to get you there and build the company from the ground up in 2 weeks and get you selling high ticket and surpassing $10K in the remaining 4. That's what I can do (but only for now, because this is no way to scale, but I already thought about that).

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: I'm putting together something especial...

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: After we finish up the first phase of my program I realized that I'm building from the ground up new companies... coaching businesses that could grow infinitely with the right guidence and information but above all execution. I was planning to make an upsale of $12K or so after the program but I thought... why doing that if I have built these companies with them and they trust me with their lives now?

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: Actually!

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: What if I just acquire 21% of each company (and charge also the $12K since they are already making a shitload of money anyways) and we can basically do what I was planning to do with them separately but now better... which is... applyin the scientific method to their buisinesses!

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: How this would look like?

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: When you have a business you can break it down to it's smallest components.

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: A business is simply a sales' system and product (or methodology) [as the business grows more components are added, like marketing department, client success fulfilment and many other things).

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: Of each of these components you have a version 1.0.

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: But if you break down even further those components you get... let's say for sales' system sales' script and appointment setting script of which you'd have version 1 of each.

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: You come up with a theory "This sales' script might work better than the one we have." Since you are tracking your KPIs you know your conversion rates are... let's say for example of 20%. You change the script and start trying... but to actually know if it works better or not you've got to test it across 30 reiterations minimum. And you can only test one thing at a time because if you test many you won't know what's causing the variations in the results. So it takes a looooooong time for most companies that are actually high level enough to use this apporach. But now imagine having 100 companies built with the exact same methodology and all communicating to a central matrix for coordination on what to test and what the other companies' tests unveiled... all on the same market but on different niches.

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: And this is how I'll build my monopoly over the Self-Improvement market and actually surpass these giants I just spoke about... this is how I beat Alex.

Kate Capelli.author: Lead

Kate Capelli.author: Scott
Kate Capelli.text: No worries, Kate, take your time... it's a lot but if I'm being honest it's fucking gold!
